---
title: "Transporter Investment Analysis"
output:
  html_document:
    df_print: paged
---

```{r setup, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, include=FALSE}
rm(list = ls())

############################################################
# 0) LOAD RETAINED DATA (CSV exports from QC pipeline)
#    Then reproduce transporter vs non-transporter pie charts
############################################################

suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(readr)
  library(dplyr)
  library(stringr)
  library(tidyr)
  library(ggplot2)
  library(scales)
})

setwd("/SynologyDrive/PhD/Projects/SpringBloom2021/DataAnalysis_SpringBloom2021/Figure_04/Transporter_Investment/")

# ---- Paths (edit if you put the CSVs elsewhere)
protein_path <- "../Data_Filtration/retained_protein_table.csv"
species_path <- "../Data_Filtration/retained_species.csv"
keys_path    <- "../Data_Filtration/retained_species_dates.csv"
species_summary_path <- "../../../Data/Species_Data/Final_Data/Species_Table.csv"

# ---- Load
protein_final          <- read_csv(protein_path, show_col_types = FALSE)
retained_species       <- read_csv(species_path, show_col_types = FALSE)
retained_species_dates <- read_csv(keys_path,    show_col_types = FALSE)
species_taxonomy <- read_csv(species_summary_path, show_col_types = FALSE)

species_taxonomy <- species_taxonomy %>%
  dplyr::select(Species_ID, Phylum, Class, Order, Family, Genus, Species) %>%
  distinct()

# ---- Basic cleanup / checks
protein_final <- protein_final %>% mutate(Date = as.Date(Date))
retained_species_dates <- retained_species_dates %>% mutate(Date = as.Date(Date))

stopifnot(
  all(c("Species_ID","Protein_ID","Date","Group_For_Plots") %in% names(protein_final)),
  all(c("Species_ID","Date","Group_For_Plots") %in% names(retained_species_dates))
)

cat("\n=== Loaded retained datasets ===\n")
cat("protein_final rows:", nrow(protein_final), "\n")
cat("retained species×dates:", nrow(retained_species_dates), "\n")
cat("retained species:", nrow(retained_species), "\n")
```

# Transporter fraction investigation
```{r}
############################################################
# TPF investigation 
#
# What this script does
#  1) Compute transporter proteome fraction (TPF) per Species × Date:
#       TPF = sum(abundance of transporter proteins) / sum(total protein abundance)
#     Transporters are defined as: KEGG_Subcategory contains the word "Transporters"
#
#  2) Summarise TPF per species (one value per species):
#       - mean_TPF across dates
#       - median_TPF across dates
#       - n_dates retained
#       - mean proteins detected per date (optional size covariate for plots)
#
#  3) Visualisations:
#       A) Density distributions of mean_TPF and median_TPF by trophic group (with stats text)
#       B) Scatter of mean_TPF vs TrophicIndex and median_TPF vs TrophicIndex
#          (dot size = mean proteins detected per date)
#       C) Boxplots for mean_TPF and median_TPF (outliers hidden, datapoints shown)
#          with overall + pairwise significance (ns/*/**/***/****)
#
#  4) Statistics (species-level inference):
#       - Overall: Kruskal–Wallis (non-parametric; appropriate for bounded fractions)
#       - Pairwise: Wilcoxon rank-sum with BH correction
#       - Effect sizes: rank-biserial correlation + 95% CI
#
# Inputs expected in `protein_final`:
#   Species_ID, Date, Protein_ID, KEGG_Subcategory,
#   Protein_Abundance_IBAQ_Norm,
#   TrophicGroup, TrophicIndex
############################################################

suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(dplyr)
  library(stringr)
  library(ggplot2)
  library(rstatix)  # kruskal_test(), pairwise_wilcox_test(), wilcox_effsize(), add_significance()
  library(ggpubr)   # stat_pvalue_manual()
})

############################################################
# 0) USER SETTINGS (edit here only)
############################################################

# Expected trophic group labels + plotting order
trophic_levels <- c("Oligo_like", "Intermediate", "Copio_like")

# Consistent group colors
trophic_cols <- c(
  "Oligo_like"   = "#93C2E6",
  "Intermediate" = "#EDEAE9",
  "Copio_like"   = "#BA5205"
)

# Multiple testing correction for pairwise comparisons
padj_method <- "BH"

# Optional: minimum number of valid dates per species for stable summaries
# (set to 5 if that matches your current filtering logic; 3 is a minimal default)
min_valid_dates_per_species <- 5


############################################################
# 1) HELPER FUNCTIONS (formatting + stats tables)
############################################################

# Pretty p-value printing for annotations
fmt_p <- function(p) {
  if (is.na(p)) return("NA")
  if (p < 1e-3) return("<0.001")
  formatC(p, format = "e", digits = 2)
}

# Create pairwise Wilcoxon results incl. adjusted p-values + star labels + y positions
pairwise_tests_with_y <- function(df, response_col, padj_method = "BH", step_increase = 0.08) {
  df %>%
    pairwise_wilcox_test(
      reformulate("TrophicGroup", response = response_col),
      p.adjust.method = padj_method
    ) %>%
    add_significance("p.adj") %>%               # adds p.adj.signif = ns/*/**/***/****
    add_y_position(fun = "max", step.increase = step_increase)
}

# Compute rank-biserial effect sizes and join to pairwise test table
pairwise_tests_plus_effects <- function(df, response_col, padj_method = "BH") {

  pw <- pairwise_tests_with_y(df, response_col, padj_method = padj_method)

  eff <- df %>%
    wilcox_effsize(
      reformulate("TrophicGroup", response = response_col),
      paired = FALSE,
      ci = TRUE
    ) %>%
    select(group1, group2, effsize, conf.low, conf.high)

  pw_full <- pw %>%
    left_join(eff, by = c("group1", "group2"))

  list(pw = pw, pw_full = pw_full, eff = eff)
}

# Build a small multi-line annotation string for density plots
build_density_annotation <- function(kw_obj, pw_tbl, levels_vec) {
  # expected 3-level comparisons for your trophic_levels
  a <- levels_vec[1]; b <- levels_vec[2]; c <- levels_vec[3]

  # helper to find adjusted p-value for a pair
  get_padj <- function(tbl, g1, g2) {
    p <- tbl$p.adj[(tbl$group1 == g1 & tbl$group2 == g2) | (tbl$group1 == g2 & tbl$group2 == g1)]
    if (length(p) == 0) NA_real_ else p[1]
  }

  paste0(
    "Kruskal–Wallis p = ", fmt_p(kw_obj$p.value), "\n",
    "Pairwise Wilcoxon (BH):\n",
    a, " vs ", b, ": ", fmt_p(get_padj(pw_tbl, a, b)), "\n",
    a, " vs ", c, ": ", fmt_p(get_padj(pw_tbl, a, c)), "\n",
    b, " vs ", c, ": ", fmt_p(get_padj(pw_tbl, b, c))
  )
}


############################################################
# 2) COMPUTE TPF PER SPECIES × DATE
############################################################

species_date_tpf <- protein_final %>%
  mutate(
    # Transporter definition:
    is_transporter = !is.na(KEGG_Subcategory) &
      str_detect(KEGG_Subcategory, "\\bTransporters\\b"),
    abund = Protein_Abundance_IBAQ_Norm
  ) %>%
  group_by(Species_ID, Date) %>%
  summarise(
    abund_total = sum(abund, na.rm = TRUE),
    abund_trans = sum(abund[is_transporter], na.rm = TRUE),
    TPF = abund_trans / abund_total,
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  # Keep only proper fractions and valid denominators
  filter(is.finite(TPF), abund_total > 0, TPF > 0, TPF < 1)

############################################################
# 3) PROTEIN DETECTION PER SPECIES × DATE (for point size later)
############################################################

species_date_protcount <- protein_final %>%
  group_by(Species_ID, Date) %>%
  summarise(N_prot_detected = n_distinct(Protein_ID), .groups = "drop")

############################################################
# 4) SPECIES-LEVEL SUMMARY TABLE (one row per species)
#    Join trophic traits (TrophicGroup, TrophicIndex) from protein_final.
############################################################

species_trait_df <- species_date_tpf %>%
  left_join(species_date_protcount, by = c("Species_ID", "Date")) %>%
  left_join(
    protein_final %>% distinct(Species_ID, TrophicGroup, TrophicIndex),
    by = "Species_ID"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    TrophicGroup = factor(as.character(TrophicGroup), levels = trophic_levels)
  ) %>%
  group_by(Species_ID, TrophicGroup, TrophicIndex) %>%
  summarise(
    n_dates = n(),
    mean_TPF   = mean(TPF, na.rm = TRUE),
    median_TPF = median(TPF, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_prot_detected = mean(N_prot_detected, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  # reviewer-safe sanity filters
  filter(
    n_dates >= min_valid_dates_per_species,
    is.finite(TrophicIndex),
    is.finite(mean_TPF),   mean_TPF > 0,   mean_TPF < 1,
    is.finite(median_TPF), median_TPF > 0, median_TPF < 1,
    is.finite(mean_prot_detected)
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    mean_prot_detected = as.integer(round(mean_prot_detected))
  )

# Print sample sizes (useful for reporting)
cat("\n=== Species counts per trophic group (after filtering) ===\n")
print(species_trait_df %>% count(TrophicGroup))


############################################################
# 5) STATISTICS (species-level): overall + pairwise + effects
############################################################

# Overall Kruskal–Wallis tests
kw_mean   <- kruskal.test(mean_TPF ~ TrophicGroup, data = species_trait_df)
kw_median <- kruskal.test(median_TPF ~ TrophicGroup, data = species_trait_df)

# Pairwise tests + effects (rank-biserial) for mean and median
res_mean   <- pairwise_tests_plus_effects(species_trait_df, "mean_TPF", padj_method = padj_method)
res_median <- pairwise_tests_plus_effects(species_trait_df, "median_TPF", padj_method = padj_method)

pw_mean      <- res_mean$pw
pw_mean_full <- res_mean$pw_full

pw_median      <- res_median$pw
pw_median_full <- res_median$pw_full

# Optional: print tables for supplement / reporting
cat("\n=== MEAN TPF: Kruskal–Wallis ===\n"); print(kw_mean)
cat("\n=== MEAN TPF: Pairwise Wilcoxon (BH) + effect size (rank-biserial) ===\n"); print(pw_mean_full)

cat("\n=== MEDIAN TPF: Kruskal–Wallis ===\n"); print(kw_median)
cat("\n=== MEDIAN TPF: Pairwise Wilcoxon (BH) + effect size (rank-biserial) ===\n"); print(pw_median_full)


############################################################
# 6) PLOT A: DENSITY DISTRIBUTIONS (mean_TPF and median_TPF)
############################################################

ann_mean <- build_density_annotation(kw_mean, pw_mean, trophic_levels)
ann_median <- build_density_annotation(kw_median, pw_median, trophic_levels)

p_density_mean <- ggplot(species_trait_df, aes(x = mean_TPF, fill = TrophicGroup, color = TrophicGroup)) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.25, linewidth = 1) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = trophic_cols, drop = FALSE) +
  scale_color_manual(values = trophic_cols, drop = FALSE) +
  labs(
    title = "Distribution of mean transporter proteome fraction (TPF) by trophic strategy",
    x = "Mean TPF (per species)",
    y = "Density"
  ) +
  annotate(
    "label",
    x = Inf, y = Inf, hjust = 1.02, vjust = 1.02,
    label = ann_mean, size = 3.2, label.size = 0.25
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank())

p_density_median <- ggplot(species_trait_df, aes(x = median_TPF, fill = TrophicGroup, color = TrophicGroup)) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.25, linewidth = 1) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = trophic_cols, drop = FALSE) +
  scale_color_manual(values = trophic_cols, drop = FALSE) +
  labs(
    title = "Distribution of median transporter proteome fraction (TPF) by trophic strategy",
    x = "Median TPF (per species)",
    y = "Density"
  ) +
  annotate(
    "label",
    x = Inf, y = Inf, hjust = 1.02, vjust = 1.02,
    label = ann_median, size = 3.2, label.size = 0.25
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank())

print(p_density_mean)
print(p_density_median)


############################################################
# 7) PLOT B: TPF vs TROPHIC INDEX (dot size = mean proteins detected)
############################################################

# Size legend breaks (min + 1/3 + 2/3 + max); fall back to 4 equally spaced if needed
size_breaks <- species_trait_df %>%
  summarise(
    mn = min(mean_prot_detected, na.rm = TRUE),
    q1 = as.integer(round(quantile(mean_prot_detected, 1/3, na.rm = TRUE))),
    q2 = as.integer(round(quantile(mean_prot_detected, 2/3, na.rm = TRUE))),
    mx = max(mean_prot_detected, na.rm = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  unlist(use.names = FALSE) %>% unique() %>% sort()

if (length(size_breaks) < 4) {
  mn <- min(species_trait_df$mean_prot_detected, na.rm = TRUE)
  mx <- max(species_trait_df$mean_prot_detected, na.rm = TRUE)
  size_breaks <- unique(as.integer(round(seq(mn, mx, length.out = 4))))
}

size_labels <- as.character(size_breaks)
size_labels[1] <- paste0(size_labels[1], " (min)")
size_labels[length(size_labels)] <- paste0(size_labels[length(size_labels)], " (max)")

base_scales_scatter <- list(
  scale_color_manual(values = trophic_cols, drop = FALSE),
  scale_size_continuous(
    name = "Mean proteins detected\n(distinct Protein_ID per date)",
    range = c(2, 10),
    breaks = size_breaks,
    labels = size_labels
  ),
  theme_bw(),
  theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank())
)

p_scatter_mean <- ggplot(
  species_trait_df,
  aes(x = TrophicIndex, y = mean_TPF, color = TrophicGroup, size = mean_prot_detected)
) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.85) +
  labs(
    title = "Mean transporter proteome fraction vs trophic index",
    x = "Trophic index",
    y = "Mean TPF (per species)",
    color = "Trophic group"
  ) +
  base_scales_scatter

p_scatter_median <- ggplot(
  species_trait_df,
  aes(x = TrophicIndex, y = median_TPF, color = TrophicGroup, size = mean_prot_detected)
) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.85) +
  labs(
    title = "Median transporter proteome fraction vs trophic index",
    x = "Trophic index",
    y = "Median TPF (per species)",
    color = "Trophic group"
  ) +
  base_scales_scatter

print(p_scatter_mean)
print(p_scatter_median)


############################################################
# 8) PLOT C: BOXPLOTS + POINTS + SIGNIFICANCE STARS
#    Outliers hidden, but ALL datapoints are shown.
############################################################

p_box_mean <- ggplot(species_trait_df, aes(x = TrophicGroup, y = mean_TPF, fill = TrophicGroup)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, width = 0.6, alpha = 0.65) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.15, height = 0, size = 2, alpha = 0.8) +
  stat_pvalue_manual(
    pw_mean,
    label = "p.adj.signif",
    tip.length = 0.01,
    hide.ns = FALSE
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = trophic_cols, drop = FALSE) +
  labs(
    title = "Mean TPF across trophic groups",
    subtitle = paste0("Overall: Kruskal–Wallis; Pairwise: Wilcoxon + ", padj_method, " correction"),
    x = NULL,
    y = "Mean TPF (per species)"
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(legend.position = "none", panel.grid.minor = element_blank())

p_box_median <- ggplot(species_trait_df, aes(x = TrophicGroup, y = median_TPF, fill = TrophicGroup)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, width = 0.6, alpha = 0.65) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.15, height = 0, size = 2, alpha = 0.8) +
  stat_pvalue_manual(
    pw_median,
    label = "p.adj.signif",
    tip.length = 0.01,
    hide.ns = FALSE
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = trophic_cols, drop = FALSE) +
  labs(
    title = "Median TPF across trophic groups",
    subtitle = paste0("Overall: Kruskal–Wallis; Pairwise: Wilcoxon + ", padj_method, " correction"),
    x = NULL,
    y = "Median TPF (per species)"
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(legend.position = "none", panel.grid.minor = element_blank())

print(p_box_mean)
print(p_box_median)


############################################################
# 9) OPTIONAL: Overall effect size for Kruskal–Wallis (epsilon-squared)
############################################################
cat("\n=== Kruskal–Wallis effect sizes (epsilon-squared) ===\n")
print(species_trait_df %>% kruskal_effsize(mean_TPF ~ TrophicGroup))
print(species_trait_df %>% kruskal_effsize(median_TPF ~ TrophicGroup))


############################################################
# "Species-level transporter proteome fraction (TPF) was calculated per species and date
#  as the summed abundance of transporter proteins divided by the summed abundance of
#  all detected proteins. Species-level summaries (mean and median across dates) were
#  compared among trophic groups using a Kruskal–Wallis test. Pairwise differences were
#  assessed using Wilcoxon rank-sum tests with Benjamini–Hochberg correction for multiple
#  comparisons. Boxplots show medians and IQR; outliers are hidden and individual species
#  values are overlaid. Rank-biserial correlations (95% CI) are reported as effect sizes."
############################################################
```

# test if there is a phylum, number of dates or number of protein effect on the correlation between trophic index and transporter proteome allocation
```{r}
############################################################
# TPF ~ TrophicIndex (species-level + date-level)
# mean + median, with robust controls + diagnostics + effect sizes
#
# PURPOSE (what we test):
#   We test whether transporter proteome fraction (TPF; a proportion in (0,1))
#   is inversely related to trophic index (continuous), i.e. higher transporter
#   allocation at lower trophic index, and whether this relationship is robust to:
#     - taxonomy (Phylum / Class),
#     - temporal replication (n_dates),
#     - detection depth (proteins detected).
#
# WHY THESE MODELS:
#   - Response is a fraction (0,1) -> Beta regression with logit link is appropriate.
#   - Repeated measures (species × date) -> include (1|Species_ID) in date-level model.
#   - Taxonomy can induce clustering -> include (1|Phylum) and/or (1|Class).
#   - Detection depth / replication can bias fractions -> include as covariates or weights.
#
# WHAT THIS SCRIPT OUTPUTS:
#   1) Species-level models:
#        mean_TPF ~ TrophicIndex + n_dates + detection + (1|Phylum)  [primary]
#        median_TPF ~ ...                                           [primary]
#        plus Class variants + Phylum+Class variants (robustness)
#        plus interaction test (TI × detection), plus weighted (weights=n_dates)
#   2) Date-level models (strongest inference):
#        TPF_date ~ TrophicIndex + detection + (1|Species_ID) + (1|Phylum/Class)
#   3) Diagnostics:
#        DHARMa uniformity/dispersion/outliers + VIF + R² + pdHess checks
#   4) Publication-ready figures:
#        Scatter + bias-corrected prediction ribbon + annotation with:
#           β (logit), 95% CI, p, exp(β), R²m/R²c, N
#        PLUS: predicted effect on response scale across TI (10th→90th percentile)
#   5) A plain-text report with:
#        - methods paragraph (stats)
#        - all model summaries + comparisons + diagnostics
#        - 3-sentence biological interpretation + verdict
#
# INPUTS REQUIRED:
#   protein_final: Date, Species_ID, Protein_ID, KEGG_Subcategory,
#                 Protein_Abundance_IBAQ_Norm, TrophicIndex, TrophicGroup (optional)
#   species_taxonomy: Species_ID, Phylum, Class, ...
############################################################

suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(dplyr)
  library(stringr)
  library(ggplot2)

  library(glmmTMB)
  library(DHARMa)
  library(performance)
  library(broom.mixed)
  library(ggeffects)
})

############################################################
# 0) USER SETTINGS (edit here only)
############################################################

# QC thresholds (reflect your choices; keep consistent across analyses)
min_proteins_per_date <- 10
require_trans_and_non <- TRUE
min_valid_dates_per_species <- 5

# Transporter definition
transporter_regex <- "\\bTransporters\\b"

# Prediction bias correction for GLMMs on response scale (Jensen's inequality)
use_bias_correction <- TRUE

# Output report
report_file <- "TPF_TrophicIndex_NatureReady_Report.txt"

# Optional: save figures (set TRUE to save png files)
save_figures <- FALSE
fig_prefix <- "TPF_TrophicIndex_"

############################################################
# 1) HELPERS: formatting, annotations, and effect size summaries
############################################################

fmt_p <- function(p) {
  if (is.na(p)) return("NA")
  if (p < 1e-4) return("<1e-4")
  if (p < 1e-3) return("<0.001")
  formatC(p, format = "e", digits = 2)
}

# Robust convergence check
check_convergence <- function(model) {
  list(
    pdHess = tryCatch(isTRUE(model$sdr$pdHess), error = function(e) FALSE),
    diagnose = tryCatch(glmmTMB::diagnose(model), error = function(e) NULL)
  )
}

# Extract key fixed effect (TrophicIndex_z)
extract_TI <- function(model, term = "TrophicIndex_z") {
  td <- broom.mixed::tidy(model, effects = "fixed", conf.int = TRUE)
  row <- td %>% filter(term == !!term)
  if (nrow(row) != 1) return(NULL)
  row
}

# Create compact annotation text for figures (robust to singular random effects)
model_annotation_text <- function(model, response_label, term = "TrophicIndex_z") {

  ti <- extract_TI(model, term)
  if (is.null(ti)) return("")

  beta <- ti$estimate
  ci_l <- ti$conf.low
  ci_u <- ti$conf.high
  pval <- ti$p.value

  # --- R² can be missing/partial for singular fits; handle defensively
  r2_txt <- ""
  r2 <- tryCatch(performance::r2(model), error = function(e) NULL)

  if (!is.null(r2)) {
    # Convert to numeric safely (covers NULL, NA, lists, etc.)
    r2m <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(r2$R2_marginal))
    r2c <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(r2$R2_conditional))

    has_r2m <- length(r2m) == 1 && is.finite(r2m)
    has_r2c <- length(r2c) == 1 && is.finite(r2c)

    if (has_r2m && has_r2c) {
      r2_txt <- paste0(
        "R²m=", formatC(r2m, digits = 2, format = "f"),
        ", R²c=", formatC(r2c, digits = 2, format = "f")
      )
    } else if (has_r2m && !has_r2c) {
      # Common for singular fits: only marginal R² is reliable/available
      r2_txt <- paste0(
        "R²m=", formatC(r2m, digits = 2, format = "f"),
        " (R²c unavailable; singular RE)"
      )
    } else {
      # If nothing usable, leave blank (never fail)
      r2_txt <- ""
    }
  }

  paste0(
    response_label, " ~ TI (beta/logit)\n",
    "β=", formatC(beta, digits = 3, format = "f"),
    " [", formatC(ci_l, digits = 3, format = "f"), ", ", formatC(ci_u, digits = 3, format = "f"), "]",
    ", p=", fmt_p(pval), "\n",
    "exp(β)=", formatC(exp(beta), digits = 2, format = "f"),
    " [", formatC(exp(ci_l), digits = 2, format = "f"), ", ", formatC(exp(ci_u), digits = 2, format = "f"), "]",
    if (nzchar(r2_txt)) paste0("\n", r2_txt) else ""
  )
}

# Predicted effect on response scale across TI range (10th → 90th percentile)
# We report ΔTPF and fold-change (ratio) between predicted TPF at TI10 and TI90.
predicted_effect_TI10_TI90 <- function(model, df, response_label) {

  # Use z-scale for predictions, but report effects on ORIGINAL TI scale in text.
  ti10 <- as.numeric(quantile(df$TrophicIndex, 0.10, na.rm = TRUE))
  ti90 <- as.numeric(quantile(df$TrophicIndex, 0.90, na.rm = TRUE))
  mu   <- mean(df$TrophicIndex, na.rm = TRUE)
  sdv  <- sd(df$TrophicIndex, na.rm = TRUE)

  ti10_z <- (ti10 - mu) / sdv
  ti90_z <- (ti90 - mu) / sdv

  # ggpredict uses the model formula's variables; we predict over TI_z values.
  # We request the two specific points.
  pred <- ggeffects::ggpredict(
    model,
    terms = paste0("TrophicIndex_z [", paste(c(ti10_z, ti90_z), collapse = ","), "]"),
    bias_correction = use_bias_correction
  ) %>% as.data.frame()

  # pred$x is TI_z; predicted is on response scale
  pred$TI_orig <- pred$x * sdv + mu

  # Ensure ordering: low (10th) then high (90th)
  pred <- pred %>% arrange(TI_orig)
  p10 <- pred$predicted[1]; p90 <- pred$predicted[2]

  data.frame(
    response = response_label,
    TI10 = ti10,
    TI90 = ti90,
    pred_TPF_TI10 = p10,
    pred_TPF_TI90 = p90,
    delta = p90 - p10,
    ratio = p90 / p10
  )
}

############################################################
# 2) BUILD DATE-LEVEL QC TABLES (Species × Date)
#    We calculate TPF correctly on linear scale:
#      TPF = sum(transporter abundance) / sum(total abundance)
#    Then we remove low-coverage and structurally invalid species-dates to avoid
#    unstable fractions driven by detection artefacts.
############################################################

# Flag transporters at protein-row level
protein_flagged <- protein_final %>%
  mutate(
    is_transporter = !is.na(KEGG_Subcategory) & str_detect(KEGG_Subcategory, transporter_regex),
    abund = Protein_Abundance_IBAQ_Norm
  )

# Detection + transporter presence per Species × Date
species_date_qc <- protein_flagged %>%
  group_by(Species_ID, Date) %>%
  summarise(
    N_prot_detected = n_distinct(Protein_ID),
    has_transporter = any(is_transporter, na.rm = TRUE),
    has_nontrans    = any(!is_transporter, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# Compute TPF per Species × Date (linear sums)
species_date_tpf <- protein_flagged %>%
  group_by(Species_ID, Date) %>%
  summarise(
    abund_total = sum(abund, na.rm = TRUE),
    abund_trans = sum(abund[is_transporter], na.rm = TRUE),
    TPF = abund_trans / abund_total,
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  left_join(species_date_qc, by = c("Species_ID", "Date")) %>%
  filter(
    is.finite(TPF), abund_total > 0, TPF > 0, TPF < 1,
    N_prot_detected >= min_proteins_per_date
  ) %>%
  { if (require_trans_and_non) filter(., has_transporter, has_nontrans) else . }

# Add trophic traits (species constant) + taxonomy
species_traits <- protein_final %>%
  distinct(Species_ID, TrophicIndex, TrophicGroup)

species_date_tpf <- species_date_tpf %>%
  left_join(species_traits, by = "Species_ID") %>%
  left_join(species_taxonomy %>% select(Species_ID, Phylum, Class), by = "Species_ID") %>%
  filter(is.finite(TrophicIndex), !is.na(Phylum), !is.na(Class)) %>%
  mutate(
    Phylum = factor(Phylum),
    Class  = factor(Class),
    log_prot_detected = log1p(N_prot_detected)
  )

############################################################
# 3) SPECIES-LEVEL SUMMARY (one row per species)
#    This is the trait-style analysis: each species contributes one point.
#    We compute both mean_TPF and median_TPF across valid dates.
############################################################

species_trait_df <- species_date_tpf %>%
  group_by(Species_ID, TrophicIndex, TrophicGroup, Phylum, Class) %>%
  summarise(
    n_dates = n(),
    mean_TPF   = mean(TPF, na.rm = TRUE),
    median_TPF = median(TPF, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_prot_detected = mean(N_prot_detected, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  filter(n_dates >= min_valid_dates_per_species) %>%
  mutate(
    mean_prot_detected = as.integer(round(mean_prot_detected))
  )

# Standardize predictors (stability & interpretability)
model_df_species <- species_trait_df %>%
  mutate(
    TrophicIndex_z      = as.numeric(scale(TrophicIndex)),
    n_dates_z           = as.numeric(scale(n_dates)),
    log_prot_detected_z = as.numeric(scale(log1p(mean_prot_detected)))
  )

model_df_date <- species_date_tpf %>%
  mutate(
    TrophicIndex_z      = as.numeric(scale(TrophicIndex)),
    log_prot_detected_z = as.numeric(scale(log_prot_detected))
  )

############################################################
# 4) START REPORT (plain text): methods + outputs + interpretation
############################################################

sink(report_file)

cat("TPF ~ TrophicIndex (Nature-ready) Statistical Report\n")
cat("====================================================\n\n")

cat("STATISTICAL METHODS (for manuscript)\n")
cat("------------------------------------\n")
cat(
  "Transporter proteome fraction (TPF) was calculated per species and sampling date as the summed\n",
  "normalized abundance of proteins annotated as 'Transporters' divided by the summed normalized\n",
  "abundance of all detected proteins (linear scale). To reduce unstable fraction estimates driven\n",
  "by low proteome coverage, species–date observations with < ", min_proteins_per_date,
  " detected proteins were excluded, and we additionally required at least one transporter and one\n",
  "non-transporter protein per retained species–date (", require_trans_and_non, "). Species included in\n",
  "species-level analyses were required to have ≥ ", min_valid_dates_per_species, " valid sampling dates.\n\n",
  "Because TPF is a continuous proportion bounded between 0 and 1, we used beta regression with a\n",
  "logit link (glmmTMB). Species-level trait models tested mean_TPF and median_TPF as responses with\n",
  "TrophicIndex as the predictor of interest and included covariates for temporal replication (n_dates)\n",
  "and detection depth (log1p mean proteins detected). Taxonomic structure was controlled using random\n",
  "intercepts at the Phylum and/or Class level. To confirm robustness, we performed sensitivity analyses\n",
  "including a TrophicIndex × detection-depth interaction and a weighted species-level model (weights = n_dates).\n",
  "To leverage the full time series and account for repeated measures, we additionally fit date-level mixed models\n",
  "of TPF per species–date including (1|Species_ID) and taxonomy random intercepts. Model adequacy was assessed\n",
  "using DHARMa residual simulations (uniformity, dispersion, outliers), multicollinearity checks (VIF), and\n",
  "marginal/conditional R². For visualization, adjusted predictions on the response scale used bias correction\n",
  "to mitigate Jensen’s inequality.\n\n",
  sep = ""
)

cat("\nDATA SUMMARY AFTER QC\n")
cat("---------------------\n")
cat("Date-level rows (species×date) after QC: ", nrow(model_df_date), "\n", sep = "")
cat("Species-level rows after QC: ", nrow(model_df_species), "\n\n", sep = "")

cat("Species per Phylum:\n"); print(sort(table(model_df_species$Phylum), decreasing = TRUE))
cat("\nSpecies per Class:\n");  print(sort(table(model_df_species$Class),  decreasing = TRUE))
cat("\n\n")

############################################################
# 5) SPECIES-LEVEL MODELS (MEAN + MEDIAN) WITH TAXONOMIC ROBUSTNESS
############################################################

cat("SPECIES-LEVEL MODELS\n")
cat("--------------------\n")
cat("Rationale: one datapoint per species; controls include replication and coverage; taxonomy as random intercept.\n\n")

fit_species_models <- function(response_col) {

  # Primary: Phylum random intercept
  m_phy <- glmmTMB(
    reformulate(c("TrophicIndex_z", "n_dates_z", "log_prot_detected_z", "(1|Phylum)"), response = response_col),
    family = beta_family(link = "logit"),
    data = model_df_species
  )

  # Robustness: Class random intercept
  m_cls <- glmmTMB(
    reformulate(c("TrophicIndex_z", "n_dates_z", "log_prot_detected_z", "(1|Class)"), response = response_col),
    family = beta_family(link = "logit"),
    data = model_df_species
  )

  # Extra-conservative: both
  m_phy_cls <- glmmTMB(
    reformulate(c("TrophicIndex_z", "n_dates_z", "log_prot_detected_z", "(1|Phylum)", "(1|Class)"), response = response_col),
    family = beta_family(link = "logit"),
    data = model_df_species
  )

  list(m_phy = m_phy, m_cls = m_cls, m_phy_cls = m_phy_cls)
}

mods_sp_mean   <- fit_species_models("mean_TPF")
mods_sp_median <- fit_species_models("median_TPF")

cat("\n[mean_TPF | (1|Phylum)]\n");     print(summary(mods_sp_mean$m_phy))
cat("\n[mean_TPF | (1|Class)]\n");      print(summary(mods_sp_mean$m_cls))
cat("\n[mean_TPF | (1|Phylum)+(1|Class)]\n"); print(summary(mods_sp_mean$m_phy_cls))
cat("\nAIC comparison (mean_TPF):\n");  print(AIC(mods_sp_mean$m_phy, mods_sp_mean$m_cls, mods_sp_mean$m_phy_cls))

cat("\n[median_TPF | (1|Phylum)]\n");     print(summary(mods_sp_median$m_phy))
cat("\n[median_TPF | (1|Class)]\n");      print(summary(mods_sp_median$m_cls))
cat("\n[median_TPF | (1|Phylum)+(1|Class)]\n"); print(summary(mods_sp_median$m_phy_cls))
cat("\nAIC comparison (median_TPF):\n");  print(AIC(mods_sp_median$m_phy, mods_sp_median$m_cls, mods_sp_median$m_phy_cls))

sing_sp_mean <- performance::check_singularity(mods_sp_mean$m_phy)
sing_sp_median <- performance::check_singularity(mods_sp_median$m_phy)

cat("\nSingularity check (mean_sim_bray primary):\n"); print(sing_sp_mean)
cat("\nSingularity check (median_sim_bray primary):\n"); print(sing_sp_median)

############################################################
# 6) SPECIES-LEVEL SENSITIVITY (MEAN + MEDIAN)
#    - Interaction TI × detection depth (tests whether slope depends on coverage)
#    - Weighted model (weights=n_dates) (tests robustness to unequal replication)
############################################################

cat("\nSPECIES-LEVEL SENSITIVITY ANALYSES\n")
cat("----------------------------------\n")

fit_species_sensitivity <- function(base_model, response_col) {

  # (i) Interaction
  m_int <- update(base_model, . ~ . + TrophicIndex_z:log_prot_detected_z)

  # (ii) Weighted model
  m_w <- glmmTMB(
    reformulate(c("TrophicIndex_z", "n_dates_z", "log_prot_detected_z", "(1|Phylum)"), response = response_col),
    family = beta_family(link = "logit"),
    data = model_df_species,
    weights = n_dates
  )

  list(m_int = m_int, m_w = m_w)
}

sens_sp_mean   <- fit_species_sensitivity(mods_sp_mean$m_phy,   "mean_TPF")
sens_sp_median <- fit_species_sensitivity(mods_sp_median$m_phy, "median_TPF")

cat("\nInteraction test (mean_TPF): base vs + TI×detection\n")
print(anova(mods_sp_mean$m_phy, sens_sp_mean$m_int))

cat("\nWeighted model (mean_TPF; weights=n_dates)\n")
print(summary(sens_sp_mean$m_w))

cat("\nInteraction test (median_TPF): base vs + TI×detection\n")
print(anova(mods_sp_median$m_phy, sens_sp_median$m_int))

cat("\nWeighted model (median_TPF; weights=n_dates)\n")
print(summary(sens_sp_median$m_w))

############################################################
# 7) DATE-LEVEL MIXED MODELS (MEANINGFUL REPEATED-MEASURES TEST)
#    Here each row is a species×date observation (after QC).
#    We include (1|Species_ID) to model repeated measurements.
#    We control detection depth per date, and taxonomy as random intercept.
############################################################

cat("\nDATE-LEVEL MODELS\n")
cat("-----------------\n")
cat("Rationale: uses full time series, accounts for repeated measures within species.\n\n")

fit_date_models <- function(tax_level = c("Class", "Phylum")) {
  tax_level <- match.arg(tax_level)

  re_tax <- if (tax_level == "Class") "(1|Class)" else "(1|Phylum)"

  glmmTMB(
    reformulate(c("TrophicIndex_z", "log_prot_detected_z", "(1|Species_ID)", re_tax), response = "TPF"),
    family = beta_family(link = "logit"),
    data = model_df_date
  )
}

m_dt_class <- fit_date_models("Class")
m_dt_phyl  <- fit_date_models("Phylum")

cat("\n[TPF_date | (1|Species_ID) + (1|Class)]\n");  print(summary(m_dt_class))
cat("\n[TPF_date | (1|Species_ID) + (1|Phylum)]\n"); print(summary(m_dt_phyl))

cat("\nAIC comparison (date-level):\n")
print(AIC(m_dt_class, m_dt_phyl))

############################################################
# 8) DIAGNOSTICS (MEAN + MEDIAN + DATE-LEVEL)
############################################################

cat("\nMODEL DIAGNOSTICS\n")
cat("-----------------\n")

diagnose_block <- function(model, label) {
  cat("\n", label, "\n", sep = "")
  conv <- check_convergence(model)
  cat("pdHess: ", conv$pdHess, "\n", sep = "")
  if (!is.null(conv$diagnose)) print(conv$diagnose)

  cat("\nDHARMa tests:\n")
  res <- simulateResiduals(model, n = 1000)
  print(testUniformity(res))
  print(testDispersion(res))
  print(testOutliers(res))

  cat("\nCollinearity (VIF):\n")
  print(check_collinearity(model))

  cat("\nR²:\n")
  print(performance::r2(model))
}

diagnose_block(mods_sp_mean$m_phy,   "Species-level (mean_TPF) primary model")
diagnose_block(mods_sp_median$m_phy, "Species-level (median_TPF) primary model")
diagnose_block(m_dt_class,           "Date-level primary model (Class)")

############################################################
# 9) RESPONSE-SCALE EFFECT SIZE (predicted TPF difference across TI range)
#    This converts β on logit scale into a directly interpretable change in predicted TPF.
############################################################

cat("\nPREDICTED EFFECT SIZE ON RESPONSE SCALE\n")
cat("--------------------------------------\n")
cat("We report predicted TPF at TI 10th percentile vs 90th percentile (bias-corrected predictions).\n\n")

eff_sp_mean   <- predicted_effect_TI10_TI90(mods_sp_mean$m_phy,   model_df_species, "Species mean_TPF")
eff_sp_median <- predicted_effect_TI10_TI90(mods_sp_median$m_phy, model_df_species, "Species median_TPF")
eff_dt_class  <- predicted_effect_TI10_TI90(m_dt_class,           model_df_date,    "Date-level TPF")

print(eff_sp_mean)
print(eff_sp_median)
print(eff_dt_class)

############################################################
# 10) BIOLOGICAL INTERPRETATION (3 sentences) + VERDICT
############################################################

cat("\nBIOLOGICAL INTERPRETATION (3 sentences)\n")
cat("---------------------------------------\n")
cat(
  "Across species, transporter proteome fraction declines continuously with increasing trophic index,\n",
  "indicating that taxa positioned toward the oligotrophic end allocate a larger share of their proteome to transport.\n",
  "This negative association persists after controlling for broad taxonomy (Phylum/Class), temporal replication, and proteome coverage,\n",
  "and is recovered both in trait-style (species-level) and repeated-measures (date-level) mixed models.\n",
  "Together, the results support a robust transporter-allocation trade-off along a continuous trophic strategy axis rather than\n",
  "a pattern driven by sampling depth or taxonomic composition.\n",
  sep = ""
)

cat("\nVERDICT\n")
cat("-------\n")
cat(
  "The modelling framework is appropriate for bounded fractional responses (beta regression), controls key confounders,\n",
  "and is supported by residual diagnostics and robustness checks; remaining limitations are primarily the use of taxonomy\n",
  "as a coarse phylogenetic proxy rather than an explicit phylogeny.\n",
  sep = ""
)

sink()  # end report

cat("Wrote report to: ", report_file, "\n", sep = "")

############################################################
# 11) FIGURES (MEAN + MEDIAN, species-level + date-level)
#     We show the raw data + bias-corrected adjusted predictions + stats box.
############################################################

# Species-level predictions (mean)
pred_sp_mean <- ggeffects::ggpredict(
  mods_sp_mean$m_phy,
  terms = "TrophicIndex_z [all]",
  bias_correction = use_bias_correction
) %>% as.data.frame()

# Back-transform TI to original units for x-axis
mu_sp <- mean(model_df_species$TrophicIndex, na.rm = TRUE)
sd_sp <- sd(model_df_species$TrophicIndex, na.rm = TRUE)
pred_sp_mean$TrophicIndex <- pred_sp_mean$x * sd_sp + mu_sp

ann_sp_mean <- model_annotation_text(mods_sp_mean$m_phy, "Species mean_TPF")
eff_txt_sp_mean <- paste0(
  "Pred effect (TI10→TI90): Δ=",
  formatC(eff_sp_mean$delta, digits = 3, format = "f"),
  ", ratio=",
  formatC(eff_sp_mean$ratio, digits = 2, format = "f")
)

p_species_mean <- ggplot() +
  geom_point(
    data = model_df_species,
    aes(x = TrophicIndex, y = mean_TPF),
    alpha = 0.35, size = 1.6
  ) +
  geom_ribbon(
    data = pred_sp_mean,
    aes(x = TrophicIndex, ymin = conf.low, ymax = conf.high),
    alpha = 0.18
  ) +
  geom_line(
    data = pred_sp_mean,
    aes(x = TrophicIndex, y = predicted),
    linewidth = 1
  ) +
  annotate(
    "label",
    x = Inf, y = Inf, hjust = 1.02, vjust = 1.02,
    label = paste0(
      ann_sp_mean, "\n",
      eff_txt_sp_mean, "\n",
      "N species=", nrow(model_df_species),
      "; min dates/species=", min_valid_dates_per_species,
      "\nQC: ≥", min_proteins_per_date, " proteins/date",
      if (require_trans_and_non) "; +trans & +non" else ""
    ),
    size = 3.2, label.size = 0.25
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(
    title = "Species-level inverse relationship between trophic index and transporter investment (mean_TPF)",
    subtitle = "Beta mixed model (logit) controlling detection depth + replication; taxonomy random intercept (Phylum)",
    x = "Trophic index",
    y = "Mean transporter proteome fraction (TPF)"
  ) +
  theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank())

print(p_species_mean)

# Species-level predictions (median)
pred_sp_median <- ggeffects::ggpredict(
  mods_sp_median$m_phy,
  terms = "TrophicIndex_z [all]",
  bias_correction = use_bias_correction
) %>% as.data.frame()

pred_sp_median$TrophicIndex <- pred_sp_median$x * sd_sp + mu_sp

ann_sp_median <- model_annotation_text(mods_sp_median$m_phy, "Species median_TPF")
eff_txt_sp_median <- paste0(
  "Pred effect (TI10→TI90): Δ=",
  formatC(eff_sp_median$delta, digits = 3, format = "f"),
  ", ratio=",
  formatC(eff_sp_median$ratio, digits = 2, format = "f")
)

p_species_median <- ggplot() +
  geom_point(
    data = model_df_species,
    aes(x = TrophicIndex, y = median_TPF),
    alpha = 0.35, size = 1.6
  ) +
  geom_ribbon(
    data = pred_sp_median,
    aes(x = TrophicIndex, ymin = conf.low, ymax = conf.high),
    alpha = 0.18
  ) +
  geom_line(
    data = pred_sp_median,
    aes(x = TrophicIndex, y = predicted),
    linewidth = 1
  ) +
  annotate(
    "label",
    x = Inf, y = Inf, hjust = 1.02, vjust = 1.02,
    label = paste0(
      ann_sp_median, "\n",
      eff_txt_sp_median, "\n",
      "N species=", nrow(model_df_species),
      "; min dates/species=", min_valid_dates_per_species,
      "\nQC: ≥", min_proteins_per_date, " proteins/date",
      if (require_trans_and_non) "; +trans & +non" else ""
    ),
    size = 3.2, label.size = 0.25
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(
    title = "Species-level inverse relationship between trophic index and transporter investment (median_TPF)",
    subtitle = "Beta mixed model (logit) controlling detection depth + replication; taxonomy random intercept (Phylum)",
    x = "Trophic index",
    y = "Median transporter proteome fraction (TPF)"
  ) +
  theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank())

print(p_species_median)

# Date-level predictions (primary = Class model)
pred_dt <- ggeffects::ggpredict(
  m_dt_class,
  terms = "TrophicIndex_z [all]",
  bias_correction = use_bias_correction
) %>% as.data.frame()

mu_dt <- mean(model_df_date$TrophicIndex, na.rm = TRUE)
sd_dt <- sd(model_df_date$TrophicIndex, na.rm = TRUE)
pred_dt$TrophicIndex <- pred_dt$x * sd_dt + mu_dt

ann_dt <- model_annotation_text(m_dt_class, "Date-level TPF")
eff_txt_dt <- paste0(
  "Pred effect (TI10→TI90): Δ=",
  formatC(eff_dt_class$delta, digits = 3, format = "f"),
  ", ratio=",
  formatC(eff_dt_class$ratio, digits = 2, format = "f")
)

p_date_level <- ggplot() +
  geom_point(
    data = model_df_date,
    aes(x = TrophicIndex, y = TPF),
    alpha = 0.12, size = 1.0
  ) +
  geom_ribbon(
    data = pred_dt,
    aes(x = TrophicIndex, ymin = conf.low, ymax = conf.high),
    alpha = 0.18
  ) +
  geom_line(
    data = pred_dt,
    aes(x = TrophicIndex, y = predicted),
    linewidth = 1
  ) +
  annotate(
    "label",
    x = Inf, y = Inf, hjust = 1.02, vjust = 1.02,
    label = paste0(
      ann_dt, "\n",
      eff_txt_dt, "\n",
      "N species-date=", nrow(model_df_date),
      "; N species=", dplyr::n_distinct(model_df_date$Species_ID),
      "\nRandom: (1|Species_ID) + (1|Class)",
      "\nQC: ≥", min_proteins_per_date, " proteins/date",
      if (require_trans_and_non) "; +trans & +non" else ""
    ),
    size = 3.2, label.size = 0.25
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(
    title = "Date-level inverse relationship between trophic index and transporter investment",
    subtitle = "Beta mixed model with repeated measures; bias-corrected adjusted predictions",
    x = "Trophic index",
    y = "Transporter proteome fraction (TPF) per species-date"
  ) +
  theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank())

print(p_date_level)

############################################################
# 12) OPTIONAL: SAVE FIGURES
############################################################
if (save_figures) {
  ggsave(paste0(fig_prefix, "species_mean.png"),   p_species_mean,   width = 7.5, height = 5.2, dpi = 300)
  ggsave(paste0(fig_prefix, "species_median.png"), p_species_median, width = 7.5, height = 5.2, dpi = 300)
  ggsave(paste0(fig_prefix, "date_level.png"),     p_date_level,     width = 7.5, height = 5.2, dpi = 300)
}

cat("Done.\n")
```


--------------------------------------- Done with Transporter proteome fraction --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------


#####################################################################################################
# Now proteome allocation stability - TOTAL PROTEOME ------------------------------------------------
#####################################################################################################

# Variablility parameter assessment of whole proteome
```{r}
############################################################
# KEGG-PROFILE STABILITY (WHOLE PROTEOME)
# — consecutive-date similarity on KEGG functional intensity profiles
# — INTENSITY-SENSITIVE (no relative normalization)
#
# What this script does
#  1) Build KEGG intensity profiles per Species × Date:
#       kegg_abund(KEGG_ID) = sum(Protein_Abundance_IBAQ_Norm) over proteins with that KEGG_ID
#     (whole proteome = all KEGG IDs; excludes NA KEGG_ID)
#     NOTE: if multiple proteins share the same KEGG_ID on a date, they are summed (via summarise(sum)).
#
#  2) Apply QC logic at Species×Date and Species level (UNCHANGED):
#       - keep Species×Date with >= min_proteins_per_date (distinct Protein_ID)
#       - optionally require at least 1 KEGG transporter and 1 non-transporter present
#       - keep Species with >= min_valid_dates_per_species after QC
#
#  3) For each Species:
#       - order QC-passed dates
#       - compute similarity between consecutive dates for KEGG profiles using:
#           A) Bray–Curtis similarity:        S = 1 − BC distance on absolute KEGG intensities
#           B) Sqrt(abs)+Euclidean similarity S = 1/(1 + d_Euclid( sqrt(absolute KEGG intensities) ))
#
#  4) Summarize per species (one row per species):
#       - mean_similarity across transitions
#       - median_similarity across transitions
#       - n_transitions
#       - mean proteins detected per date (for point size)
#
#  5) Same figures/tests per method:
#       - Density (mean + median similarity) by trophic group + stats text
#       - Scatter vs TrophicIndex (size = mean proteins detected)
#       - Boxplots (outliers hidden, points shown) + KW + pairwise Wilcoxon BH + effect sizes
############################################################

suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(dplyr)
  library(stringr)
  library(tidyr)
  library(ggplot2)

  library(vegan)
  library(rstatix)
  library(ggpubr)
})

############################################################
# 0) USER SETTINGS
############################################################

trophic_levels <- c("Oligo_like", "Intermediate", "Copio_like")

trophic_cols <- c(
  "Oligo_like"   = "#93C2E6",
  "Intermediate" = "#EDEAE9",
  "Copio_like"   = "#BA5205"
)

padj_method <- "BH"

min_proteins_per_date <- 10
min_valid_dates_per_species <- 5

# QC option (kept as in your original): require both transporter + non-transporter KEGG evidence per Species×Date
require_trans_and_non_kegg <- TRUE
transporter_regex <- "\\bTransporters\\b"

############################################################
# 1) HELPERS
############################################################

fmt_p <- function(p) {
  if (is.na(p)) return("NA")
  if (p < 1e-3) return("<0.001")
  formatC(p, format = "e", digits = 2)
}

pairwise_tests_with_y <- function(df, response_col, padj_method = "BH", step_increase = 0.08) {
  df %>%
    pairwise_wilcox_test(
      reformulate("TrophicGroup", response = response_col),
      p.adjust.method = padj_method
    ) %>%
    add_significance("p.adj") %>%
    add_y_position(fun = "max", step.increase = step_increase)
}

pairwise_tests_plus_effects <- function(df, response_col, padj_method = "BH") {
  pw <- pairwise_tests_with_y(df, response_col, padj_method = padj_method)

  eff <- df %>%
    wilcox_effsize(
      reformulate("TrophicGroup", response = response_col),
      paired = FALSE,
      ci = TRUE
    ) %>%
    select(group1, group2, effsize, conf.low, conf.high)

  pw_full <- pw %>%
    left_join(eff, by = c("group1", "group2"))

  list(pw = pw, pw_full = pw_full, eff = eff)
}

build_density_annotation <- function(kw_obj, pw_tbl, levels_vec) {
  a <- levels_vec[1]; b <- levels_vec[2]; c <- levels_vec[3]
  get_padj <- function(tbl, g1, g2) {
    p <- tbl$p.adj[(tbl$group1 == g1 & tbl$group2 == g2) | (tbl$group1 == g2 & tbl$group2 == g1)]
    if (length(p) == 0) NA_real_ else p[1]
  }
  paste0(
    "Kruskal–Wallis p = ", fmt_p(kw_obj$p.value), "\n",
    "Pairwise Wilcoxon (BH):\n",
    a, " vs ", b, ": ", fmt_p(get_padj(pw_tbl, a, b)), "\n",
    a, " vs ", c, ": ", fmt_p(get_padj(pw_tbl, a, c)), "\n",
    b, " vs ", c, ": ", fmt_p(get_padj(pw_tbl, b, c))
  )
}

############################################################
# 2) QC: retain species×date with enough proteins + KEGG present
############################################################

species_date_protcount <- protein_final %>%
  group_by(Species_ID, Date) %>%
  summarise(N_prot_detected = n_distinct(Protein_ID), .groups = "drop")

species_date_kegg_presence <- protein_final %>%
  mutate(
    has_kegg = !is.na(KEGG_ID),
    is_transporter_kegg = has_kegg & !is.na(KEGG_Subcategory) & str_detect(KEGG_Subcategory, transporter_regex)
  ) %>%
  group_by(Species_ID, Date) %>%
  summarise(
    has_any_kegg = any(has_kegg),
    has_trans_kegg = any(is_transporter_kegg, na.rm = TRUE),
    has_nontrans_kegg = any(has_kegg & !is_transporter_kegg, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

species_date_keep <- species_date_protcount %>%
  left_join(species_date_kegg_presence, by = c("Species_ID", "Date")) %>%
  filter(
    N_prot_detected >= min_proteins_per_date,
    has_any_kegg
  ) %>%
  { if (require_trans_and_non_kegg) filter(., has_trans_kegg, has_nontrans_kegg) else . }

species_keep <- species_date_keep %>%
  count(Species_ID, name = "n_dates") %>%
  filter(n_dates >= min_valid_dates_per_species)

species_date_keep <- species_date_keep %>%
  inner_join(species_keep %>% select(Species_ID), by = "Species_ID")

species_traits <- protein_final %>%
  distinct(Species_ID, TrophicGroup, TrophicIndex)

species_date_keep <- species_date_keep %>%
  left_join(species_traits, by = "Species_ID") %>%
  mutate(TrophicGroup = factor(as.character(TrophicGroup), levels = trophic_levels)) %>%
  filter(is.finite(TrophicIndex), !is.na(TrophicGroup))

cat("\n=== Species retained after QC ===\n")
print(species_date_keep %>% distinct(Species_ID, TrophicGroup) %>% count(TrophicGroup))

############################################################
# 2b) OVERVIEW PLOT: distribution of consecutive-date pair counts per species
############################################################

paircount_df <- species_date_keep %>%
  distinct(Species_ID, Date) %>%
  count(Species_ID, name = "n_dates") %>%
  mutate(n_pairs = pmax(n_dates - 1, 0)) %>%
  left_join(species_date_keep %>% distinct(Species_ID, TrophicGroup), by = "Species_ID")

p_paircount <- ggplot(paircount_df, aes(x = n_pairs, fill = TrophicGroup)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 1, boundary = -0.5, alpha = 0.75, color = "grey30") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = trophic_cols, drop = FALSE) +
  labs(
    title = "Distribution of consecutive-date pair counts per species (after QC)",
    subtitle = "n_pairs = n_dates − 1, where n_dates are QC-passed dates per species",
    x = "Number of consecutive-date pairs per species",
    y = "Number of species",
    fill = "Trophic group"
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank())

print(p_paircount)

############################################################
# 3) KEGG INTENSITY PROFILES (WHOLE PROTEOME; sum per KEGG_ID per species-date)
############################################################

kegg_sd <- protein_final %>%
  filter(!is.na(KEGG_ID)) %>%  # whole proteome: keep all annotated KEGG IDs
  inner_join(species_date_keep %>% select(Species_ID, Date), by = c("Species_ID", "Date")) %>%
  group_by(Species_ID, Date, KEGG_ID) %>%
  summarise(kegg_abund = sum(Protein_Abundance_IBAQ_Norm, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  filter(is.finite(kegg_abund), kegg_abund > 0)

############################################################
# 4) CONSECUTIVE-DATE SIMILARITIES PER SPECIES (INTENSITY-SENSITIVE)
############################################################

compute_species_consecutive_similarity <- function(sp_id, kegg_sd_tbl, keep_tbl) {

  dates <- keep_tbl %>%
    filter(Species_ID == sp_id) %>%
    distinct(Date) %>%
    arrange(Date) %>%
    pull(Date)

  if (length(dates) < 2) return(NULL)

  mat_wide <- kegg_sd_tbl %>%
    filter(Species_ID == sp_id) %>%
    mutate(Date = as.character(Date)) %>%
    tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = KEGG_ID, values_from = kegg_abund, values_fill = 0) %>%
    filter(Date %in% as.character(dates)) %>%
    arrange(match(Date, as.character(dates)))

  M <- as.matrix(mat_wide %>% select(-Species_ID, -Date))
  rownames(M) <- mat_wide$Date

  # Sqrt-transform on absolute abundances (keeps intensity, downweights extremes)
  M_sqrt <- sqrt(M)

  pairs <- data.frame(Date1 = dates[-length(dates)], Date2 = dates[-1], stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

  out <- lapply(seq_len(nrow(pairs)), function(i) {
    d1 <- as.character(pairs$Date1[i])
    d2 <- as.character(pairs$Date2[i])

    # A) Bray–Curtis similarity on ABSOLUTE KEGG intensities: S = 1 − BC
    bc <- vegan::vegdist(M[c(d1, d2), , drop = FALSE], method = "bray")
    sim_bc <- 1 - as.numeric(bc)

    # B) Sqrt(abs) + Euclidean similarity: S = 1/(1 + d)
    d_sqrt <- dist(M_sqrt[c(d1, d2), , drop = FALSE], method = "euclidean")
    sim_sqrt <- 1 / (1 + as.numeric(d_sqrt))

    data.frame(
      Species_ID = sp_id,
      Date1 = pairs$Date1[i],
      Date2 = pairs$Date2[i],
      sim_bray = sim_bc,
      sim_hellinger = sim_sqrt,  # kept name for minimal downstream changes
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    )
  })

  bind_rows(out)
}

all_species <- sort(unique(species_date_keep$Species_ID))

sim_transitions <- bind_rows(lapply(
  all_species,
  compute_species_consecutive_similarity,
  kegg_sd_tbl = kegg_sd,
  keep_tbl = species_date_keep
))

species_covars <- species_date_keep %>%
  group_by(Species_ID, TrophicGroup, TrophicIndex) %>%
  summarise(
    n_dates = n(),
    mean_prot_detected = mean(N_prot_detected, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(mean_prot_detected = as.integer(round(mean_prot_detected)))

sim_transitions <- sim_transitions %>%
  left_join(species_covars, by = "Species_ID")

############################################################
# 5) SPECIES-LEVEL SUMMARY (mean + median similarity per species, per method)
############################################################

species_stability <- sim_transitions %>%
  group_by(Species_ID, TrophicGroup, TrophicIndex, n_dates, mean_prot_detected) %>%
  summarise(
    n_transitions = n(),
    mean_sim_bray = mean(sim_bray, na.rm = TRUE),
    median_sim_bray = median(sim_bray, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_sim_hell = mean(sim_hellinger, na.rm = TRUE),
    median_sim_hell = median(sim_hellinger, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(TrophicGroup = factor(as.character(TrophicGroup), levels = trophic_levels))

############################################################
# 6) RUN SAME PLOTS + TESTS PER METHOD
############################################################

run_method_suite <- function(df, method_short, method_definition, mean_col, median_col) {

  method_full <- paste0(method_short, " — ", method_definition)

  cat("\n\n############################################################\n")
  cat("### METHOD:", method_full, "\n")
  cat("############################################################\n")

  kw_mean   <- kruskal.test(reformulate("TrophicGroup", response = mean_col), data = df)
  kw_median <- kruskal.test(reformulate("TrophicGroup", response = median_col), data = df)

  res_mean   <- pairwise_tests_plus_effects(df, mean_col, padj_method = padj_method)
  res_median <- pairwise_tests_plus_effects(df, median_col, padj_method = padj_method)

  pw_mean   <- res_mean$pw
  pw_median <- res_median$pw

  ann_mean   <- build_density_annotation(kw_mean,   pw_mean,   trophic_levels)
  ann_median <- build_density_annotation(kw_median, pw_median, trophic_levels)

  # Density: mean
  p_den_mean <- ggplot(df, aes(x = .data[[mean_col]], fill = TrophicGroup, color = TrophicGroup)) +
    geom_density(alpha = 0.25, linewidth = 1) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = trophic_cols, drop = FALSE) +
    scale_color_manual(values = trophic_cols, drop = FALSE) +
    labs(
      title = paste0(method_full, "\nMean consecutive-date similarity (per species)"),
      x = "Mean similarity",
      y = "Density"
    ) +
    annotate("label", x = Inf, y = Inf, hjust = 1.02, vjust = 1.02,
             label = ann_mean, size = 3.2, label.size = 0.25) +
    theme_bw() +
    theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank())

  # Density: median
  p_den_median <- ggplot(df, aes(x = .data[[median_col]], fill = TrophicGroup, color = TrophicGroup)) +
    geom_density(alpha = 0.25, linewidth = 1) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = trophic_cols, drop = FALSE) +
    scale_color_manual(values = trophic_cols, drop = FALSE) +
    labs(
      title = paste0(method_full, "\nMedian consecutive-date similarity (per species)"),
      x = "Median similarity",
      y = "Density"
    ) +
    annotate("label", x = Inf, y = Inf, hjust = 1.02, vjust = 1.02,
             label = ann_median, size = 3.2, label.size = 0.25) +
    theme_bw() +
    theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank())

  print(p_den_mean)
  print(p_den_median)

  # Scatter: size breaks
  size_breaks <- df %>%
    summarise(
      mn = min(mean_prot_detected, na.rm = TRUE),
      q1 = as.integer(round(quantile(mean_prot_detected, 1/3, na.rm = TRUE))),
      q2 = as.integer(round(quantile(mean_prot_detected, 2/3, na.rm = TRUE))),
      mx = max(mean_prot_detected, na.rm = TRUE)
    ) %>%
    unlist(use.names = FALSE) %>% unique() %>% sort()

  if (length(size_breaks) < 4) {
    mn <- min(df$mean_prot_detected, na.rm = TRUE)
    mx <- max(df$mean_prot_detected, na.rm = TRUE)
    size_breaks <- unique(as.integer(round(seq(mn, mx, length.out = 4))))
  }

  size_labels <- as.character(size_breaks)
  size_labels[1] <- paste0(size_labels[1], " (min)")
  size_labels[length(size_labels)] <- paste0(size_labels[length(size_labels)], " (max)")

  base_scales_scatter <- list(
    scale_color_manual(values = trophic_cols, drop = FALSE),
    scale_size_continuous(
      name = "Mean proteins detected\n(distinct Protein_ID per date)",
      range = c(2, 10),
      breaks = size_breaks,
      labels = size_labels
    ),
    theme_bw(),
    theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank())
  )

  p_sc_mean <- ggplot(df, aes(x = TrophicIndex, y = .data[[mean_col]], color = TrophicGroup, size = mean_prot_detected)) +
    geom_point(alpha = 0.85) +
    labs(
      title = paste0(method_full, "\nMean similarity vs trophic index"),
      x = "Trophic index",
      y = "Mean similarity",
      color = "Trophic group"
    ) +
    base_scales_scatter

  p_sc_median <- ggplot(df, aes(x = TrophicIndex, y = .data[[median_col]], color = TrophicGroup, size = mean_prot_detected)) +
    geom_point(alpha = 0.85) +
    labs(
      title = paste0(method_full, "\nMedian similarity vs trophic index"),
      x = "Trophic index",
      y = "Median similarity",
      color = "Trophic group"
    ) +
    base_scales_scatter

  print(p_sc_mean)
  print(p_sc_median)

  # Boxplots
  p_box_mean <- ggplot(df, aes(x = TrophicGroup, y = .data[[mean_col]], fill = TrophicGroup)) +
    geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, width = 0.6, alpha = 0.65) +
    geom_jitter(width = 0.15, height = 0, size = 2, alpha = 0.8) +
    stat_pvalue_manual(pw_mean, label = "p.adj.signif", tip.length = 0.01, hide.ns = FALSE) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = trophic_cols, drop = FALSE) +
    labs(
      title = paste0(method_full, "\nMean similarity across trophic groups"),
      subtitle = paste0("Overall: Kruskal–Wallis; Pairwise: Wilcoxon + ", padj_method),
      x = NULL, y = "Mean similarity"
    ) +
    theme_bw() +
    theme(legend.position = "none", panel.grid.minor = element_blank())

  p_box_median <- ggplot(df, aes(x = TrophicGroup, y = .data[[median_col]], fill = TrophicGroup)) +
    geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, width = 0.6, alpha = 0.65) +
    geom_jitter(width = 0.15, height = 0, size = 2, alpha = 0.8) +
    stat_pvalue_manual(pw_median, label = "p.adj.signif", tip.length = 0.01, hide.ns = FALSE) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = trophic_cols, drop = FALSE) +
    labs(
      title = paste0(method_full, "\nMedian similarity across trophic groups"),
      subtitle = paste0("Overall: Kruskal–Wallis; Pairwise: Wilcoxon + ", padj_method),
      x = NULL, y = "Median similarity"
    ) +
    theme_bw() +
    theme(legend.position = "none", panel.grid.minor = element_blank())

  print(p_box_mean)
  print(p_box_median)

  invisible(list(
    kw_mean = kw_mean, kw_median = kw_median,
    pw_mean_full = res_mean$pw_full,
    pw_median_full = res_median$pw_full
  ))
}

############################################################
# 7) RUN BOTH METHODS (INTENSITY-SENSITIVE)
############################################################

res_bray <- run_method_suite(
  species_stability,
  method_short = "Bray–Curtis similarity",
  method_definition = "S = 1 − BC distance on absolute KEGG intensity profiles (summed iBAQ)",
  mean_col = "mean_sim_bray",
  median_col = "median_sim_bray"
)

res_hell <- run_method_suite(
  species_stability,
  method_short = "Sqrt(abs) + Euclidean similarity",
  method_definition = "S = 1/(1 + Euclidean distance) on sqrt(absolute KEGG intensity profiles)",
  mean_col = "mean_sim_hell",
  median_col = "median_sim_hell"
)

############################################################
# "Whole-proteome functional stability was quantified per species as the similarity between
#  consecutive sampling dates of KEGG functional intensity profiles. For each species×date, protein
#  intensities (iBAQ-normalized abundances) were summed per KEGG ID (multiple proteins with the same
#  KEGG ID were summed) to form a KEGG intensity vector. Similarity between consecutive dates was
#  computed using Bray–Curtis similarity (1−BC distance) on absolute KEGG intensities and a square-
#  root–transformed Euclidean similarity (1/(1+d)). Species-level mean and median similarities were
#  compared among trophic groups using Kruskal–Wallis tests with pairwise Wilcoxon rank-sum tests and
#  BH correction; rank-biserial correlations (95% CI) were used as effect sizes. Boxplots show medians
#  and IQR with outliers hidden and individual species values overlaid."
############################################################
```


# Variablility parameter assessment of transporter proteome
```{r}
############################################################
# KEGG-PROFILE STABILITY (TRANSPORTER PROTEOME ONLY)
# — consecutive-date similarity on transporter-only KEGG profiles
# — INTENSITY-SENSITIVE (no relative normalization)
#
# What this script does
#  1) QC (unchanged): keep Species×Date with enough proteins + KEGG present,
#     optionally require ≥1 transporter KEGG and ≥1 non-transporter KEGG per date,
#     then keep Species with enough valid dates.
#
#  2) Build transporter-only KEGG intensity profiles per Species×Date:
#       kegg_abund(KEGG_ID) = sum(Protein_Abundance_IBAQ_Norm)
#     This automatically sums multiple proteins mapping to the same KEGG_ID on the same date.
#
#  3) For each Species, compute similarity between consecutive QC-passed dates:
#       A) Bray–Curtis similarity on absolute KEGG intensities:      S = 1 − BC(M_abs)
#       B) Sqrt(Hellinger-like) + Euclidean similarity on abs data:  S = 1/(1 + d_Euclid(sqrt(M_abs)))
#     (No decostand / proportions anywhere; total intensity is preserved.)
#
#  4) Summarize per species (mean/median similarity across transitions),
#     then produce the exact same figures + stats suite for both measures.
############################################################

suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(dplyr)
  library(stringr)
  library(tidyr)
  library(ggplot2)

  library(vegan)
  library(rstatix)
  library(ggpubr)
})

############################################################
# 0) USER SETTINGS
############################################################

trophic_levels <- c("Oligo_like", "Intermediate", "Copio_like")

trophic_cols <- c(
  "Oligo_like"   = "#93C2E6",
  "Intermediate" = "#EDEAE9",
  "Copio_like"   = "#BA5205"
)

padj_method <- "BH"

min_proteins_per_date <- 10
min_valid_dates_per_species <- 5

require_trans_and_non_kegg <- TRUE
transporter_regex <- "\\bTransporters\\b"

############################################################
# 1) HELPERS
############################################################

fmt_p <- function(p) {
  if (is.na(p)) return("NA")
  if (p < 1e-3) return("<0.001")
  formatC(p, format = "e", digits = 2)
}

pairwise_tests_with_y <- function(df, response_col, padj_method = "BH", step_increase = 0.08) {
  df %>%
    pairwise_wilcox_test(
      reformulate("TrophicGroup", response = response_col),
      p.adjust.method = padj_method
    ) %>%
    add_significance("p.adj") %>%
    add_y_position(fun = "max", step.increase = step_increase)
}

pairwise_tests_plus_effects <- function(df, response_col, padj_method = "BH") {
  pw <- pairwise_tests_with_y(df, response_col, padj_method = padj_method)

  eff <- df %>%
    wilcox_effsize(
      reformulate("TrophicGroup", response = response_col),
      paired = FALSE,
      ci = TRUE
    ) %>%
    select(group1, group2, effsize, conf.low, conf.high)

  pw_full <- pw %>%
    left_join(eff, by = c("group1", "group2"))

  list(pw = pw, pw_full = pw_full, eff = eff)
}

build_density_annotation <- function(kw_obj, pw_tbl, levels_vec) {
  a <- levels_vec[1]; b <- levels_vec[2]; c <- levels_vec[3]
  get_padj <- function(tbl, g1, g2) {
    p <- tbl$p.adj[(tbl$group1 == g1 & tbl$group2 == g2) | (tbl$group1 == g2 & tbl$group2 == g1)]
    if (length(p) == 0) NA_real_ else p[1]
  }
  paste0(
    "Kruskal–Wallis p = ", fmt_p(kw_obj$p.value), "\n",
    "Pairwise Wilcoxon (BH):\n",
    a, " vs ", b, ": ", fmt_p(get_padj(pw_tbl, a, b)), "\n",
    a, " vs ", c, ": ", fmt_p(get_padj(pw_tbl, a, c)), "\n",
    b, " vs ", c, ": ", fmt_p(get_padj(pw_tbl, b, c))
  )
}

############################################################
# 2) QC: retain species×date with enough proteins + KEGG present
############################################################

species_date_protcount <- protein_final %>%
  group_by(Species_ID, Date) %>%
  summarise(N_prot_detected = n_distinct(Protein_ID), .groups = "drop")

species_date_kegg_presence <- protein_final %>%
  mutate(
    has_kegg = !is.na(KEGG_ID),
    is_transporter_kegg = has_kegg & !is.na(KEGG_Subcategory) & str_detect(KEGG_Subcategory, transporter_regex)
  ) %>%
  group_by(Species_ID, Date) %>%
  summarise(
    has_any_kegg = any(has_kegg),
    has_trans_kegg = any(is_transporter_kegg, na.rm = TRUE),
    has_nontrans_kegg = any(has_kegg & !is_transporter_kegg, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

species_date_keep <- species_date_protcount %>%
  left_join(species_date_kegg_presence, by = c("Species_ID", "Date")) %>%
  filter(
    N_prot_detected >= min_proteins_per_date,
    has_any_kegg
  ) %>%
  { if (require_trans_and_non_kegg) filter(., has_trans_kegg, has_nontrans_kegg) else . }

species_keep <- species_date_keep %>%
  count(Species_ID, name = "n_dates") %>%
  filter(n_dates >= min_valid_dates_per_species)

species_date_keep <- species_date_keep %>%
  inner_join(species_keep %>% select(Species_ID), by = "Species_ID")

species_traits <- protein_final %>%
  distinct(Species_ID, TrophicGroup, TrophicIndex)

species_date_keep <- species_date_keep %>%
  left_join(species_traits, by = "Species_ID") %>%
  mutate(TrophicGroup = factor(as.character(TrophicGroup), levels = trophic_levels)) %>%
  filter(is.finite(TrophicIndex), !is.na(TrophicGroup))

cat("\n=== Species retained after QC ===\n")
print(species_date_keep %>% distinct(Species_ID, TrophicGroup) %>% count(TrophicGroup))

############################################################
# 2b) OVERVIEW PLOT: distribution of consecutive-date pair counts per species
############################################################

paircount_df <- species_date_keep %>%
  distinct(Species_ID, Date) %>%
  count(Species_ID, name = "n_dates") %>%
  mutate(n_pairs = pmax(n_dates - 1, 0)) %>%
  left_join(species_date_keep %>% distinct(Species_ID, TrophicGroup), by = "Species_ID")

p_paircount <- ggplot(paircount_df, aes(x = n_pairs, fill = TrophicGroup)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 1, boundary = -0.5, alpha = 0.75, color = "grey30") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = trophic_cols, drop = FALSE) +
  labs(
    title = "Distribution of consecutive-date pair counts per species (after QC)",
    subtitle = "n_pairs = n_dates − 1, where n_dates are QC-passed dates per species",
    x = "Number of consecutive-date pairs per species",
    y = "Number of species",
    fill = "Trophic group"
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank())

print(p_paircount)

############################################################
# 3) KEGG INTENSITY PROFILES (transporter-only; sum per KEGG_ID per species-date)
############################################################

kegg_sd <- protein_final %>%
  filter(
    !is.na(KEGG_ID),
    !is.na(KEGG_Subcategory),
    str_detect(KEGG_Subcategory, transporter_regex)
  ) %>%
  inner_join(species_date_keep %>% select(Species_ID, Date), by = c("Species_ID", "Date")) %>%
  group_by(Species_ID, Date, KEGG_ID) %>%
  summarise(kegg_abund = sum(Protein_Abundance_IBAQ_Norm, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  filter(is.finite(kegg_abund), kegg_abund > 0)

############################################################
# 4) CONSECUTIVE-DATE SIMILARITIES PER SPECIES (INTENSITY-SENSITIVE)
############################################################

compute_species_consecutive_similarity <- function(sp_id, kegg_sd_tbl, keep_tbl) {

  dates <- keep_tbl %>%
    filter(Species_ID == sp_id) %>%
    distinct(Date) %>%
    arrange(Date) %>%
    pull(Date)

  if (length(dates) < 2) return(NULL)

  mat_wide <- kegg_sd_tbl %>%
    filter(Species_ID == sp_id) %>%
    mutate(Date = as.character(Date)) %>%
    tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = KEGG_ID, values_from = kegg_abund, values_fill = 0) %>%
    filter(Date %in% as.character(dates)) %>%
    arrange(match(Date, as.character(dates)))

  M <- as.matrix(mat_wide %>% select(-Species_ID, -Date))
  rownames(M) <- mat_wide$Date

  # Sqrt-transform on absolute abundances (keeps intensity, downweights extremes)
  M_sqrt <- sqrt(M)

  pairs <- data.frame(Date1 = dates[-length(dates)], Date2 = dates[-1], stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

  out <- lapply(seq_len(nrow(pairs)), function(i) {
    d1 <- as.character(pairs$Date1[i])
    d2 <- as.character(pairs$Date2[i])

    # A) Bray–Curtis similarity on ABSOLUTE KEGG intensities: S = 1 − BC
    bc <- vegan::vegdist(M[c(d1, d2), , drop = FALSE], method = "bray")
    sim_bc <- 1 - as.numeric(bc)

    # B) Sqrt(abs) + Euclidean similarity: S = 1/(1 + d)
    d_sqrt <- dist(M_sqrt[c(d1, d2), , drop = FALSE], method = "euclidean")
    sim_sqrt <- 1 / (1 + as.numeric(d_sqrt))

    data.frame(
      Species_ID = sp_id,
      Date1 = pairs$Date1[i],
      Date2 = pairs$Date2[i],
      sim_bray = sim_bc,
      sim_hellinger = sim_sqrt,  # name kept for minimal downstream changes
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    )
  })

  bind_rows(out)
}

all_species <- sort(unique(species_date_keep$Species_ID))

sim_transitions <- bind_rows(lapply(
  all_species,
  compute_species_consecutive_similarity,
  kegg_sd_tbl = kegg_sd,
  keep_tbl = species_date_keep
))

species_covars <- species_date_keep %>%
  group_by(Species_ID, TrophicGroup, TrophicIndex) %>%
  summarise(
    n_dates = n(),
    mean_prot_detected = mean(N_prot_detected, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(mean_prot_detected = as.integer(round(mean_prot_detected)))

sim_transitions <- sim_transitions %>%
  left_join(species_covars, by = "Species_ID")

############################################################
# 5) SPECIES-LEVEL SUMMARY (mean + median similarity per species, per method)
############################################################

species_stability <- sim_transitions %>%
  group_by(Species_ID, TrophicGroup, TrophicIndex, n_dates, mean_prot_detected) %>%
  summarise(
    n_transitions = n(),
    mean_sim_bray = mean(sim_bray, na.rm = TRUE),
    median_sim_bray = median(sim_bray, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_sim_hell = mean(sim_hellinger, na.rm = TRUE),
    median_sim_hell = median(sim_hellinger, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(TrophicGroup = factor(as.character(TrophicGroup), levels = trophic_levels))

############################################################
# 6) RUN SAME PLOTS + TESTS PER METHOD
############################################################

run_method_suite <- function(df, method_short, method_definition, mean_col, median_col) {

  method_full <- paste0(method_short, " — ", method_definition)

  cat("\n\n############################################################\n")
  cat("### METHOD:", method_full, "\n")
  cat("############################################################\n")

  kw_mean   <- kruskal.test(reformulate("TrophicGroup", response = mean_col), data = df)
  kw_median <- kruskal.test(reformulate("TrophicGroup", response = median_col), data = df)

  res_mean   <- pairwise_tests_plus_effects(df, mean_col, padj_method = padj_method)
  res_median <- pairwise_tests_plus_effects(df, median_col, padj_method = padj_method)

  pw_mean   <- res_mean$pw
  pw_median <- res_median$pw

  ann_mean   <- build_density_annotation(kw_mean,   pw_mean,   trophic_levels)
  ann_median <- build_density_annotation(kw_median, pw_median, trophic_levels)

  # Density: mean
  p_den_mean <- ggplot(df, aes(x = .data[[mean_col]], fill = TrophicGroup, color = TrophicGroup)) +
    geom_density(alpha = 0.25, linewidth = 1) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = trophic_cols, drop = FALSE) +
    scale_color_manual(values = trophic_cols, drop = FALSE) +
    labs(
      title = paste0(method_full, "\nMean consecutive-date similarity (per species)"),
      x = "Mean similarity",
      y = "Density"
    ) +
    annotate("label", x = Inf, y = Inf, hjust = 1.02, vjust = 1.02,
             label = ann_mean, size = 3.2, label.size = 0.25) +
    theme_bw() +
    theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank())

  # Density: median
  p_den_median <- ggplot(df, aes(x = .data[[median_col]], fill = TrophicGroup, color = TrophicGroup)) +
    geom_density(alpha = 0.25, linewidth = 1) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = trophic_cols, drop = FALSE) +
    scale_color_manual(values = trophic_cols, drop = FALSE) +
    labs(
      title = paste0(method_full, "\nMedian consecutive-date similarity (per species)"),
      x = "Median similarity",
      y = "Density"
    ) +
    annotate("label", x = Inf, y = Inf, hjust = 1.02, vjust = 1.02,
             label = ann_median, size = 3.2, label.size = 0.25) +
    theme_bw() +
    theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank())

  print(p_den_mean)
  print(p_den_median)

  # Scatter: size breaks
  size_breaks <- df %>%
    summarise(
      mn = min(mean_prot_detected, na.rm = TRUE),
      q1 = as.integer(round(quantile(mean_prot_detected, 1/3, na.rm = TRUE))),
      q2 = as.integer(round(quantile(mean_prot_detected, 2/3, na.rm = TRUE))),
      mx = max(mean_prot_detected, na.rm = TRUE)
    ) %>%
    unlist(use.names = FALSE) %>% unique() %>% sort()

  if (length(size_breaks) < 4) {
    mn <- min(df$mean_prot_detected, na.rm = TRUE)
    mx <- max(df$mean_prot_detected, na.rm = TRUE)
    size_breaks <- unique(as.integer(round(seq(mn, mx, length.out = 4))))
  }

  size_labels <- as.character(size_breaks)
  size_labels[1] <- paste0(size_labels[1], " (min)")
  size_labels[length(size_labels)] <- paste0(size_labels[length(size_labels)], " (max)")

  base_scales_scatter <- list(
    scale_color_manual(values = trophic_cols, drop = FALSE),
    scale_size_continuous(
      name = "Mean proteins detected\n(distinct Protein_ID per date)",
      range = c(2, 10),
      breaks = size_breaks,
      labels = size_labels
    ),
    theme_bw(),
    theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank())
  )

  p_sc_mean <- ggplot(df, aes(x = TrophicIndex, y = .data[[mean_col]], color = TrophicGroup, size = mean_prot_detected)) +
    geom_point(alpha = 0.85) +
    labs(
      title = paste0(method_full, "\nMean similarity vs trophic index"),
      x = "Trophic index",
      y = "Mean similarity",
      color = "Trophic group"
    ) +
    base_scales_scatter

  p_sc_median <- ggplot(df, aes(x = TrophicIndex, y = .data[[median_col]], color = TrophicGroup, size = mean_prot_detected)) +
    geom_point(alpha = 0.85) +
    labs(
      title = paste0(method_full, "\nMedian similarity vs trophic index"),
      x = "Trophic index",
      y = "Median similarity",
      color = "Trophic group"
    ) +
    base_scales_scatter

  print(p_sc_mean)
  print(p_sc_median)

  # Boxplots
  p_box_mean <- ggplot(df, aes(x = TrophicGroup, y = .data[[mean_col]], fill = TrophicGroup)) +
    geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, width = 0.6, alpha = 0.65) +
    geom_jitter(width = 0.15, height = 0, size = 2, alpha = 0.8) +
```
