```{r}
    stat_pvalue_manual(pw_mean, label = "p.adj.signif", tip.length = 0.01, hide.ns = FALSE) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = trophic_cols, drop = FALSE) +
    labs(
      title = paste0(method_full, "\nMean similarity across trophic groups"),
      subtitle = paste0("Overall: Kruskal–Wallis; Pairwise: Wilcoxon + ", padj_method),
      x = NULL, y = "Mean similarity"
    ) +
    theme_bw() +
    theme(legend.position = "none", panel.grid.minor = element_blank())

  p_box_median <- ggplot(df, aes(x = TrophicGroup, y = .data[[median_col]], fill = TrophicGroup)) +
    geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, width = 0.6, alpha = 0.65) +
    geom_jitter(width = 0.15, height = 0, size = 2, alpha = 0.8) +
    stat_pvalue_manual(pw_median, label = "p.adj.signif", tip.length = 0.01, hide.ns = FALSE) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = trophic_cols, drop = FALSE) +
    labs(
      title = paste0(method_full, "\nMedian similarity across trophic groups"),
      subtitle = paste0("Overall: Kruskal–Wallis; Pairwise: Wilcoxon + ", padj_method),
      x = NULL, y = "Median similarity"
    ) +
    theme_bw() +
    theme(legend.position = "none", panel.grid.minor = element_blank())

  print(p_box_mean)
  print(p_box_median)

  invisible(list(
    kw_mean = kw_mean, kw_median = kw_median,
    pw_mean_full = res_mean$pw_full,
    pw_median_full = res_median$pw_full
  ))
}

############################################################
# 7) RUN BOTH METHODS (INTENSITY-SENSITIVE)
############################################################

res_bray <- run_method_suite(
  species_stability,
  method_short = "Bray–Curtis similarity",
  method_definition = "S = 1 − BC distance on absolute KEGG intensity profiles (summed iBAQ)",
  mean_col = "mean_sim_bray",
  median_col = "median_sim_bray"
)

res_hell <- run_method_suite(
  species_stability,
  method_short = "Sqrt(abs) + Euclidean similarity",
  method_definition = "S = 1/(1 + Euclidean distance) on sqrt(absolute KEGG intensity profiles)",
  mean_col = "mean_sim_hell",
  median_col = "median_sim_hell"
)

############################################################
# "Transporter-proteome functional stability was quantified per species as the similarity between
#  consecutive sampling dates of transporter-only KEGG functional intensity profiles. For each
#  species×date, protein intensities (iBAQ-normalized abundances) were summed per KEGG ID using only
#  proteins annotated as transporters (KEGG subcategory matching 'Transporters') to form a KEGG
#  intensity vector (multiple proteins with the same KEGG ID were summed). Similarity between
#  consecutive dates was computed using Bray–Curtis similarity (1−BC distance) on absolute KEGG
#  intensities and a square-root–transformed Euclidean similarity (1/(1+d)). Species-level mean and
#  median similarities were compared among trophic groups using Kruskal–Wallis tests with pairwise
#  Wilcoxon rank-sum tests and BH correction; rank-biserial correlations (95% CI) were used as effect
#  sizes. Boxplots show medians and IQR with outliers hidden and individual species values overlaid."
############################################################
```

# test if there is a phylum, number of dates or number of protein effect on the correlation between trophic index and transporter proteome variability
```{r}
############################################################
# Transporter functional stability ~ TrophicIndex
# Bray–Curtis ONLY on ABSOLUTE intensities
#
# What this script does
# 1) QC (unchanged logic):
#    - keep Species×Date with >= min_proteins_per_date (distinct Protein_ID)
#    - require at least 1 transporter KEGG + 1 non-transporter KEGG if require_trans_and_non_kegg = TRUE
#    - keep Species with >= min_valid_dates_per_species after QC
#
# 2) Build transporter-only KEGG intensity profiles per Species×Date:
#    - filter proteins to transporter-annotated rows (KEGG_Subcategory contains "Transporters")
#    - require KEGG_ID present and KEGG_Subcategory not NA (removes "unclassified")
#    - sum Protein_Abundance_IBAQ_Norm per (Species_ID, Date, KEGG_ID)
#      -> if multiple proteins map to the same KEGG_ID on the same date, they are correctly SUMMED.
#
# 3) Compute consecutive-date Bray–Curtis on ABSOLUTE intensity profiles:
#    - BC_abs distance in [0,1]
#    - Similarity S_abs = 1 - BC_abs
#
# 4) Test TI relationship without beta regression:
#    A) Transition-level mixed model (primary inference; repeated measures):
#         logit(S_abs) ~ TrophicIndex + detection + (1|Species_ID) + (1|Class or Phylum)
#    B) Species-level models (supporting; one point per species):
#         logit(mean_S_abs)   ~ TrophicIndex + n_transitions + detection + (1|Class or Phylum)
#         logit(median_S_abs) ~ TrophicIndex + n_transitions + detection + (1|Class or Phylum)
#
# 5) Output:
#    - QC pair-count histogram
#    - 3 plots with regression ribbon + stats box:
#        transition-level S_abs vs TI
#        species-level mean S_abs vs TI
#        species-level median S_abs vs TI
#    - full stats + diagnostics report written to:
#        Transporter_BrayCurtis_ABS_ONLY_TrophicIndex_stats.txt
#
# Inputs required:
#   protein_final: Species_ID, Date, Protein_ID, KEGG_ID, KEGG_Subcategory,
#                 Protein_Abundance_IBAQ_Norm, TrophicIndex (TrophicGroup optional)
#   species_taxonomy: Species_ID, Phylum, Class
############################################################

suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(dplyr)
  library(stringr)
  library(tidyr)
  library(ggplot2)

  library(vegan)

  library(glmmTMB)
  library(DHARMa)
  library(performance)
  library(broom.mixed)
  library(ggeffects)
})

############################################################
# 0) USER SETTINGS
############################################################
min_proteins_per_date <- 10
min_valid_dates_per_species <- 5

require_trans_and_non_kegg <- TRUE
transporter_regex <- "\\bTransporters\\b"

# squeeze away from 0/1 before logit transform
eps <- 1e-6

use_bias_correction <- TRUE
report_file <- "Transporter_BrayCurtis_ABS_ONLY_TrophicIndex_stats.txt"

save_figures <- FALSE
fig_prefix <- "TransporterBCabs_TI_"

############################################################
# 1) HELPERS
############################################################
fmt_p <- function(p) {
  if (is.na(p)) return("NA")
  if (p < 1e-4) return("<1e-4")
  if (p < 1e-3) return("<0.001")
  formatC(p, format = "e", digits = 2)
}

logit_similarity <- function(S, eps = 1e-6) {
  S2 <- pmin(pmax(S, eps), 1 - eps)
  qlogis(S2)
}

extract_TI <- function(model, term = "TrophicIndex_z") {
  td <- broom.mixed::tidy(model, effects = "fixed", conf.int = TRUE)
  row <- td %>% filter(term == !!term)
  if (nrow(row) != 1) return(NULL)
  row
}

model_annotation_text <- function(model, response_label, term = "TrophicIndex_z") {
  ti <- extract_TI(model, term)
  if (is.null(ti)) return("")

  beta <- ti$estimate
  ci_l <- ti$conf.low
  ci_u <- ti$conf.high
  pval <- ti$p.value

  r2_txt <- ""
  r2 <- tryCatch(performance::r2(model), error = function(e) NULL)
  if (!is.null(r2)) {
    r2m <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(r2$R2_marginal))
    r2c <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(r2$R2_conditional))
    has_r2m <- length(r2m) == 1 && is.finite(r2m)
    has_r2c <- length(r2c) == 1 && is.finite(r2c)
    if (has_r2m && has_r2c) {
      r2_txt <- paste0("R²m=", formatC(r2m, digits = 2, format = "f"),
                       ", R²c=", formatC(r2c, digits = 2, format = "f"))
    } else if (has_r2m) {
      r2_txt <- paste0("R²m=", formatC(r2m, digits = 2, format = "f"))
    }
  }

  paste0(
    response_label, " ~ TI (logit similarity)\n",
    "β=", formatC(beta, digits = 3, format = "f"),
    " [", formatC(ci_l, digits = 3, format = "f"), ", ", formatC(ci_u, digits = 3, format = "f"), "]",
    ", p=", fmt_p(pval), "\n",
    "exp(β)=", formatC(exp(beta), digits = 2, format = "f"),
    " [", formatC(exp(ci_l), digits = 2, format = "f"), ", ", formatC(exp(ci_u), digits = 2, format = "f"), "]",
    if (nzchar(r2_txt)) paste0("\n", r2_txt) else ""
  )
}

diagnose_block <- function(model, label, n_sim = 500) {
  cat("\n", label, "\n", sep = "")
  cat(strrep("-", nchar(label)), "\n", sep = "")

  cat("pdHess: ", tryCatch(isTRUE(model$sdr$pdHess), error = function(e) NA), "\n", sep = "")

  cat("\nVIF:\n"); print(tryCatch(check_collinearity(model), error = function(e) e$message))
  cat("\nR²:\n");  print(tryCatch(performance::r2(model), error = function(e) e$message))

  cat("\nDHARMa:\n")
  res <- tryCatch(simulateResiduals(model, n = n_sim), error = function(e) NULL)
  if (is.null(res)) {
    cat("DHARMa failed (can happen for complex RE structures).\n")
  } else {
    print(testUniformity(res))
    print(testDispersion(res))
    print(testOutliers(res))
  }
}

pick_best <- function(m_class, m_phy) {
  a <- AIC(m_class); b <- AIC(m_phy)
  if (is.finite(a) && is.finite(b) && a <= b) m_class else m_phy
}

predict_curve_to_similarity <- function(model, df, ti_var = "TrophicIndex", z_var = "TrophicIndex_z") {
  pred <- ggeffects::ggpredict(
    model,
    terms = paste0(z_var, " [all]"),
    bias_correction = use_bias_correction
  ) %>% as.data.frame()

  mu <- mean(df[[ti_var]], na.rm = TRUE)
  sdv <- sd(df[[ti_var]], na.rm = TRUE)

  pred$TrophicIndex <- pred$x * sdv + mu
  pred$S <- plogis(pred$predicted)
  pred$S_low <- plogis(pred$conf.low)
  pred$S_high <- plogis(pred$conf.high)
  pred
}

############################################################
# 2) QC: SAME AS BEFORE
############################################################

species_date_protcount <- protein_final %>%
  group_by(Species_ID, Date) %>%
  summarise(N_prot_detected = n_distinct(Protein_ID), .groups = "drop")

species_date_kegg_presence <- protein_final %>%
  mutate(
    has_kegg = !is.na(KEGG_ID),
    is_transporter_kegg = has_kegg & !is.na(KEGG_Subcategory) & str_detect(KEGG_Subcategory, transporter_regex)
  ) %>%
  group_by(Species_ID, Date) %>%
  summarise(
    has_any_kegg = any(has_kegg),
    has_trans_kegg = any(is_transporter_kegg, na.rm = TRUE),
    has_nontrans_kegg = any(has_kegg & !is_transporter_kegg, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

species_date_keep <- species_date_protcount %>%
  left_join(species_date_kegg_presence, by = c("Species_ID","Date")) %>%
  filter(N_prot_detected >= min_proteins_per_date, has_any_kegg) %>%
  { if (require_trans_and_non_kegg) filter(., has_trans_kegg, has_nontrans_kegg) else . }

species_keep <- species_date_keep %>%
  count(Species_ID, name = "n_dates") %>%
  filter(n_dates >= min_valid_dates_per_species) %>%
  select(Species_ID)

species_date_keep <- species_date_keep %>%
  semi_join(species_keep, by = "Species_ID")

species_traits <- protein_final %>%
  distinct(Species_ID, TrophicIndex, TrophicGroup)

species_date_keep <- species_date_keep %>%
  left_join(species_traits, by = "Species_ID") %>%
  left_join(species_taxonomy %>% select(Species_ID, Phylum, Class), by = "Species_ID") %>%
  filter(is.finite(TrophicIndex), !is.na(Phylum), !is.na(Class)) %>%
  mutate(Phylum = factor(Phylum), Class = factor(Class))

cat("\nQC-passed species×date rows: ", nrow(species_date_keep), "\n", sep = "")
cat("QC-passed species: ", dplyr::n_distinct(species_date_keep$Species_ID), "\n", sep = "")

############################################################
# 2b) OVERVIEW PLOT: pair counts per species
############################################################
paircount_df <- species_date_keep %>%
  distinct(Species_ID, Date) %>%
  count(Species_ID, name = "n_dates") %>%
  mutate(n_pairs = pmax(n_dates - 1, 0))

p_paircount <- ggplot(paircount_df, aes(x = n_pairs)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 1, boundary = -0.5, alpha = 0.75, color = "grey30") +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank()) +
  labs(
    title = "Consecutive-date pair counts per species (after QC)",
    subtitle = "n_pairs = n_dates − 1 using QC-passed dates",
    x = "Number of consecutive-date pairs per species",
    y = "Number of species"
  )
print(p_paircount)

############################################################
# 3) TRANSPORTER KEGG INTENSITY PROFILES (Species×Date×KEGG_ID)
############################################################
kegg_sd_trans <- protein_final %>%
  filter(
    !is.na(KEGG_ID),
    !is.na(KEGG_Subcategory),
    str_detect(KEGG_Subcategory, transporter_regex)
  ) %>%
  inner_join(species_date_keep %>% select(Species_ID, Date), by = c("Species_ID","Date")) %>%
  group_by(Species_ID, Date, KEGG_ID) %>%
  summarise(
    kegg_abund = sum(Protein_Abundance_IBAQ_Norm, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  filter(is.finite(kegg_abund), kegg_abund > 0)

############################################################
# 4) TRANSITION-LEVEL BRAY–CURTIS (ABSOLUTE ONLY)
############################################################
compute_species_transitions_bc <- function(sp_id, kegg_tbl, keep_tbl) {

  dates <- keep_tbl %>%
    filter(Species_ID == sp_id) %>%
    distinct(Date) %>%
    arrange(Date) %>%
    pull(Date)

  if (length(dates) < 2) return(NULL)

  mat_wide <- kegg_tbl %>%
    filter(Species_ID == sp_id) %>%
    mutate(Date = as.character(Date)) %>%
    pivot_wider(names_from = KEGG_ID, values_from = kegg_abund, values_fill = 0) %>%
    filter(Date %in% as.character(dates)) %>%
    arrange(match(Date, as.character(dates)))

  M_abs <- as.matrix(mat_wide %>% select(-Species_ID, -Date))
  rownames(M_abs) <- mat_wide$Date

  pairs <- data.frame(Date1 = dates[-length(dates)], Date2 = dates[-1], stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

  out <- lapply(seq_len(nrow(pairs)), function(i) {
    d1 <- as.character(pairs$Date1[i]); d2 <- as.character(pairs$Date2[i])

    bc_abs <- as.numeric(vegdist(M_abs[c(d1, d2), , drop = FALSE], method = "bray"))
    s_abs <- 1 - bc_abs

    nd1 <- keep_tbl %>% filter(Species_ID == sp_id, Date == pairs$Date1[i]) %>% pull(N_prot_detected)
    nd2 <- keep_tbl %>% filter(Species_ID == sp_id, Date == pairs$Date2[i]) %>% pull(N_prot_detected)
    det_pair <- mean(c(nd1, nd2), na.rm = TRUE)

    data.frame(
      Species_ID = sp_id,
      Date1 = pairs$Date1[i],
      Date2 = pairs$Date2[i],
      bc_abs = bc_abs,
      s_abs  = s_abs,
      N_prot_pair = det_pair,
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    )
  })

  bind_rows(out)
}

all_species <- sort(unique(species_date_keep$Species_ID))

transitions <- bind_rows(lapply(
  all_species, compute_species_transitions_bc,
  kegg_tbl = kegg_sd_trans,
  keep_tbl = species_date_keep
))

transitions <- transitions %>%
  left_join(
    species_date_keep %>% distinct(Species_ID, TrophicIndex, Phylum, Class),
    by = "Species_ID"
  ) %>%
  filter(is.finite(s_abs), is.finite(N_prot_pair)) %>%
  mutate(
    log_prot_pair = log1p(N_prot_pair),
    s_abs_logit = logit_similarity(s_abs, eps = eps),
    TrophicIndex_z  = as.numeric(scale(TrophicIndex)),
    log_prot_pair_z = as.numeric(scale(log_prot_pair))
  )

############################################################
# 5) SPECIES-LEVEL SUMMARY (ABSOLUTE mean + median)
############################################################
species_level <- transitions %>%
  group_by(Species_ID, TrophicIndex, Phylum, Class) %>%
  summarise(
    n_transitions = n(),
    mean_s_abs   = mean(s_abs, na.rm = TRUE),
    median_s_abs = median(s_abs, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_prot_pair = mean(N_prot_pair, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    log_prot_pair = log1p(mean_prot_pair),
    mean_s_abs_logit   = logit_similarity(mean_s_abs, eps = eps),
    median_s_abs_logit = logit_similarity(median_s_abs, eps = eps),
    TrophicIndex_z  = as.numeric(scale(TrophicIndex)),
    n_transitions_z = as.numeric(scale(n_transitions)),
    log_prot_pair_z = as.numeric(scale(log_prot_pair))
  )

############################################################
# 6) MODELS + REPORT
############################################################
sink(report_file)

cat("Transporter stability ~ TrophicIndex (Bray–Curtis ABSOLUTE ONLY)\n")
cat("==============================================================\n\n")

cat("QC SUMMARY\n")
cat("----------\n")
cat("Transitions: ", nrow(transitions), "\n", sep = "")
cat("Species: ", nrow(species_level), "\n\n", sep = "")

cat("MEASURE\n")
cat("-------\n")
cat(
  "Bray–Curtis was computed on ABSOLUTE transporter KEGG intensity profiles per species×date.\n",
  "Similarity is S_abs = 1 - BC_abs.\n",
  "We model logit(S_abs) using Gaussian mixed models (no beta regression).\n\n",
  sep = ""
)

# Transition-level models (primary inference)
fit_transition <- function(tax = c("Class","Phylum")) {
  tax <- match.arg(tax)
  re_tax <- if (tax == "Class") "(1|Class)" else "(1|Phylum)"

  glmmTMB(
    reformulate(c("TrophicIndex_z", "log_prot_pair_z", "(1|Species_ID)", re_tax), response = "s_abs_logit"),
    family = gaussian(),
    data = transitions
  )
}

m_tr_class <- fit_transition("Class")
m_tr_phy   <- fit_transition("Phylum")

cat("\nTRANSITION-LEVEL (PRIMARY) MODELS\n")
cat("--------------------------------\n")
cat("\n[Transition | logit(S_abs) | (1|Species_ID)+(1|Class)]\n");  print(summary(m_tr_class))
cat("\n[Transition | logit(S_abs) | (1|Species_ID)+(1|Phylum)]\n"); print(summary(m_tr_phy))
cat("\nAIC compare (transition):\n"); print(AIC(m_tr_class, m_tr_phy))

# Species-level models (supporting)
fit_species <- function(resp, tax = c("Class","Phylum")) {
  tax <- match.arg(tax)
  re_tax <- if (tax == "Class") "(1|Class)" else "(1|Phylum)"

  glmmTMB(
    reformulate(c("TrophicIndex_z", "n_transitions_z", "log_prot_pair_z", re_tax), response = resp),
    family = gaussian(),
    data = species_level
  )
}

m_sp_mean_class   <- fit_species("mean_s_abs_logit", "Class")
m_sp_mean_phy     <- fit_species("mean_s_abs_logit", "Phylum")
m_sp_median_class <- fit_species("median_s_abs_logit", "Class")
m_sp_median_phy   <- fit_species("median_s_abs_logit", "Phylum")

cat("\nSPECIES-LEVEL (SUPPORTING) MODELS\n")
cat("---------------------------------\n")

cat("\n[Species | mean logit(S_abs) | Class]\n");  print(summary(m_sp_mean_class))
cat("\n[Species | mean logit(S_abs) | Phylum]\n"); print(summary(m_sp_mean_phy))
cat("\nAIC compare (species mean):\n"); print(AIC(m_sp_mean_class, m_sp_mean_phy))

cat("\n[Species | median logit(S_abs) | Class]\n");  print(summary(m_sp_median_class))
cat("\n[Species | median logit(S_abs) | Phylum]\n"); print(summary(m_sp_median_phy))
cat("\nAIC compare (species median):\n"); print(AIC(m_sp_median_class, m_sp_median_phy))

cat("\nDIAGNOSTICS (PRIMARY + SUPPORTING)\n")
cat("----------------------------------\n")
diagnose_block(m_tr_class, "Transition: logit(S_abs) + Class")
diagnose_block(m_sp_mean_class, "Species mean: logit(mean S_abs) + Class")
diagnose_block(m_sp_median_class, "Species median: logit(median S_abs) + Class")

sink()
cat("Wrote report to: ", report_file, "\n", sep = "")

############################################################
# 7) FIGURES (transition + species mean + species median)
############################################################

# pick best taxonomy level (AIC) for annotation/prediction
m_tr_best <- pick_best(m_tr_class, m_tr_phy)
m_sp_mean_best <- pick_best(m_sp_mean_class, m_sp_mean_phy)
m_sp_median_best <- pick_best(m_sp_median_class, m_sp_median_phy)

# Transition-level prediction (on similarity scale)
pred_tr <- predict_curve_to_similarity(m_tr_best, transitions)
ann_tr <- model_annotation_text(m_tr_best, "Transition logit(S_abs)")

p_tr <- ggplot() +
  geom_point(data = transitions, aes(x = TrophicIndex, y = s_abs), alpha = 0.12, size = 1) +
  geom_ribbon(data = pred_tr, aes(x = TrophicIndex, ymin = S_low, ymax = S_high), alpha = 0.18) +
  geom_line(data = pred_tr, aes(x = TrophicIndex, y = S), linewidth = 1) +
  annotate("label", x = Inf, y = Inf, hjust = 1.02, vjust = 1.02,
           label = paste0(
             ann_tr, "\n",
             "N transitions=", nrow(transitions),
             "; N species=", dplyr::n_distinct(transitions$Species_ID),
             "\nControls: detection + taxonomy + repeated measures"
           ),
           size = 3.2, label.size = 0.25) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank()) +
  labs(
    title = "Transporter functional stability vs trophic index (transition-level)",
    subtitle = "Bray–Curtis similarity across consecutive dates (ABSOLUTE KEGG transporter intensities)",
    x = "Trophic index",
    y = "Consecutive-date Bray–Curtis similarity (S_abs = 1−BC_abs)"
  )
print(p_tr)

# Species-level mean prediction
pred_sp_mean <- predict_curve_to_similarity(m_sp_mean_best, species_level)
ann_sp_mean <- model_annotation_text(m_sp_mean_best, "Species mean logit(S_abs)")

p_sp_mean <- ggplot() +
  geom_point(data = species_level, aes(x = TrophicIndex, y = mean_s_abs), alpha = 0.45, size = 1.8) +
  geom_ribbon(data = pred_sp_mean, aes(x = TrophicIndex, ymin = S_low, ymax = S_high), alpha = 0.18) +
  geom_line(data = pred_sp_mean, aes(x = TrophicIndex, y = S), linewidth = 1) +
  annotate("label", x = Inf, y = Inf, hjust = 1.02, vjust = 1.02,
           label = paste0(
             ann_sp_mean, "\n",
             "N species=", nrow(species_level),
             "\nControls: n_transitions + detection + taxonomy"
           ),
           size = 3.2, label.size = 0.25) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank()) +
  labs(
    title = "Transporter functional stability vs trophic index (species-level mean)",
    subtitle = "Mean S_abs across transitions per species",
    x = "Trophic index",
    y = "Mean consecutive-date Bray–Curtis similarity (S_abs)"
  )
print(p_sp_mean)

# Species-level median prediction
pred_sp_med <- predict_curve_to_similarity(m_sp_median_best, species_level)
ann_sp_med <- model_annotation_text(m_sp_median_best, "Species median logit(S_abs)")

p_sp_med <- ggplot() +
  geom_point(data = species_level, aes(x = TrophicIndex, y = median_s_abs), alpha = 0.45, size = 1.8) +
  geom_ribbon(data = pred_sp_med, aes(x = TrophicIndex, ymin = S_low, ymax = S_high), alpha = 0.18) +
  geom_line(data = pred_sp_med, aes(x = TrophicIndex, y = S), linewidth = 1) +
  annotate("label", x = Inf, y = Inf, hjust = 1.02, vjust = 1.02,
           label = paste0(
             ann_sp_med, "\n",
             "N species=", nrow(species_level),
             "\nControls: n_transitions + detection + taxonomy"
           ),
           size = 3.2, label.size = 0.25) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank()) +
  labs(
    title = "Transporter functional stability vs trophic index (species-level median)",
    subtitle = "Median S_abs across transitions per species",
    x = "Trophic index",
    y = "Median consecutive-date Bray–Curtis similarity (S_abs)"
  )
print(p_sp_med)

############################################################
# 8) OPTIONAL: SAVE FIGURES
############################################################
if (save_figures) {
  ggsave(paste0(fig_prefix, "paircount.png"), p_paircount, width = 7.2, height = 4.6, dpi = 300)
  ggsave(paste0(fig_prefix, "transition.png"), p_tr, width = 7.5, height = 5.2, dpi = 300)
  ggsave(paste0(fig_prefix, "species_mean.png"), p_sp_mean, width = 7.5, height = 5.2, dpi = 300)
  ggsave(paste0(fig_prefix, "species_median.png"), p_sp_med, width = 7.5, height = 5.2, dpi = 300)
}

cat("Done. Stats written to: ", report_file, "\n", sep = "")
```

# Interpretation

Below is a concise, structured comparison of the two analytical frameworks:

- **Transporter Proteome Fraction (TPF)**
- **Transporter Functional Stability (absolute Bray–Curtis similarity)**

All interpretations are based on the statistical reports:
- TPF report :contentReference[oaicite:0]{index=0}  
- Absolute Bray–Curtis report :contentReference[oaicite:1]{index=1}  

---

# I. Transporter Proteome Fraction (TPF)

## What was tested

TPF quantifies *how much* of the proteome is allocated to transporters.  
It was modeled using beta mixed models appropriate for bounded proportions.

Three complementary tests were performed:

1. **Species-level mean TPF**
2. **Species-level median TPF**
3. **Date-level repeated-measures TPF**

All models controlled for:

- Taxonomy (Phylum/Class random effects)
- Number of dates per species
- Proteome detection depth

## What the tests show

Across all levels:

- **TrophicIndex_z is significantly negative**
- Effect sizes are consistent across mean, median, and date-level models
- Results remain robust under weighting and interaction tests
- Model diagnostics are acceptable

From predicted effects (10th → 90th TI percentile):

- Species mean TPF drops from ~0.51 → ~0.27  
- Date-level TPF drops from ~0.53 → ~0.20  

This represents roughly a **40–60% reduction in transporter allocation** across the trophic gradient.

## Interpretation

Transporter investment declines continuously with increasing trophic index.

This pattern:

- Persists after controlling for taxonomy
- Persists after correcting for detection depth
- Persists under repeated-measures modeling

Thus, transporter allocation behaves as a **continuous trophic strategy trait**, not a taxonomic artifact.

---

# II. Transporter Functional Stability (Absolute Bray–Curtis)

## What was tested

Here, the question changes:

Not *how much* transporter investment exists, but  
**how stable transporter functional composition is across consecutive dates.**

Bray–Curtis similarity was computed on:

- Absolute KEGG transporter intensity profiles
- Per species × date
- Between consecutive dates

Models used Gaussian mixed models on logit-transformed similarity.

Three levels:

1. Transition-level (primary test; repeated measures)
2. Species-level mean similarity
3. Species-level median similarity

Controls:

- Taxonomy
- Number of transitions
- Detection depth per pair

## What the tests show

Across all levels:

- **TrophicIndex_z is significantly negative**
- The effect is consistent in transition-level and species-level models
- R² values indicate moderate explanatory power
- Diagnostics are acceptable (minor deviations at transition-level, expected given large N)

Thus:

Higher trophic index → lower transporter stability  
Lower trophic index → higher transporter stability

This indicates that oligotrophic taxa maintain more temporally stable transporter functional profiles.

## Interpretation

Transporter functional composition becomes increasingly dynamic toward higher trophic strategies.

Importantly, this pattern holds:

- After correcting for detection depth
- After correcting for number of transitions
- After accounting for repeated measures
- After controlling for taxonomy

Therefore, it is unlikely to be driven by sampling depth or phylogenetic clustering.

---

# III. Combined Biological Interpretation

Together, the two analyses reveal a coherent trophic strategy gradient:

### 1. Allocation effect (TPF)

Oligotrophic taxa:
- Invest more proteome mass into transporters

Copiotrophic taxa:
- Allocate less proteome mass to transport

### 2. Stability effect (Bray–Curtis)

Oligotrophic taxa:
- Maintain stable transporter functional composition over time

Copiotrophic taxa:
- Exhibit higher temporal turnover in transporter profiles

---

# IV. Overall Biological Synthesis

The joint evidence supports a **continuous transporter-centered trophic strategy axis**:

- Low trophic index taxa combine **high transporter investment** with **functional stability**.
- High trophic index taxa combine **lower transporter allocation** with **higher functional plasticity**.

This suggests:

Oligotrophic organisms adopt a **stable, transport-optimized proteomic architecture**,  
whereas copiotrophic organisms adopt a **dynamic, flexible transporter repertoire**.

Crucially, both allocation and stability signals:

- Survive correction for taxonomy
- Survive correction for sampling depth
- Persist at both species-level and repeated-measures level

Thus, transporter allocation and stability appear to be fundamental ecological design principles linked to trophic strategy rather than artifacts of phylogeny or detection bias.

---

# Final Assessment

The two independent analytical frameworks converge on the same biological gradient.

The evidence supports:

- A robust, continuous trophic trade-off
- A proteome-level ecological axis
- A mechanistically interpretable transporter strategy shift

The combined result substantially strengthens the ecological inference beyond either analysis alone.


# median tpf vs median bray curtis dissimilarity
```{r}
############################################################
# MEDIAN vs MEDIAN ONLY
# - x: median TPF per species
# - y: median variability = median Bray–Curtis dissimilarity
#      (lower = more stable; higher = more variable)
# - Hardcoded axes: 0..1 by 0.25 for scatter + boxplots
# - Same plotting style + highlight legend + PDF export only
############################################################

suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(dplyr)
  library(stringr)
  library(tidyr)
  library(ggplot2)
  library(scales)
  library(ggExtra)
  library(grid)
  library(gridExtra)
  library(readr)
  library(vegan)
})

# =========================================================
# 0) Safety checks — objects expected from your load block
# =========================================================
required_objects <- c("protein_final", "retained_species_dates", "species_taxonomy")
for (obj in required_objects) {
  if (!exists(obj, inherits = TRUE)) stop("Object not found in environment: ", obj)
}

req_keys_cols <- c("Species_ID","Date","Group_For_Plots")
missing_keys <- setdiff(req_keys_cols, names(retained_species_dates))
if (length(missing_keys) > 0) stop("retained_species_dates missing: ", paste(missing_keys, collapse=", "))

req_tax_cols <- c("Species_ID","Phylum","Genus")
missing_tax <- setdiff(req_tax_cols, names(species_taxonomy))
if (length(missing_tax) > 0) stop("species_taxonomy missing: ", paste(missing_tax, collapse=", "))

req_prot_cols <- c("Species_ID","Date","Group_For_Plots","Protein_Abundance_IBAQ_Norm","KEGG_Subcategory","KEGG_ID")
missing_prot <- setdiff(req_prot_cols, names(protein_final))
if (length(missing_prot) > 0) stop("protein_final missing: ", paste(missing_prot, collapse=", "))

protein_id_col <- dplyr::case_when(
  "Protein_Group" %in% names(protein_final) ~ "Protein_Group",
  "Protein_ID"    %in% names(protein_final) ~ "Protein_ID",
  TRUE ~ NA_character_
)
if (is.na(protein_id_col)) stop("protein_final must have Protein_Group or Protein_ID for counting detected proteins.")

protein_final <- protein_final %>% mutate(Date = as.Date(Date))
retained_species_dates <- retained_species_dates %>% mutate(Date = as.Date(Date))

# =========================================================
# 1) Fixed settings (orders, colors, highlight scheme)
# =========================================================
trophic_order <- c("Oligo_like", "Intermediate", "Copio_like")
trophic_cols <- c(
  "Oligo_like"   = "#93C2E6",
  "Intermediate" = "#EDEAE9",
  "Copio_like"   = "#BA5205"
)

highlight_cols <- c(
  "Flavobacterium"        = "#E6BE1E",
  "Planktophila"          = "#ADD5E4",
  "Fonsibacter"           = "#C6FC05",
  "Planctomycetota (phy)" = "#7B3294",
  "Rest"                  = "grey"
)
highlight_levels <- names(highlight_cols)

axis_breaks_01 <- seq(0, 1, by = 0.25)

fmt_p <- function(p) {
  if (is.na(p)) return("NA")
  if (p < 1e-3) "<0.001" else format(p, digits = 2, scientific = TRUE)
}

get_legend <- function(p) {
  g <- ggplotGrob(p)
  idx <- which(sapply(g$grobs, function(x) x$name) == "guide-box")
  if (length(idx) != 1) stop("Legend extraction failed.")
  g$grobs[[idx]]
}

export_grob_pdf <- function(g, base, width, height) {
  pdf(paste0(base, ".pdf"), width = width, height = height)
  grid.newpage(); grid.draw(g)
  dev.off()
  cat("\nSaved PDF:\n  ", normalizePath(paste0(base, ".pdf")), "\n", sep="")
}

# =========================================================
# 2) Enforce Species×Date×Group universe (critical)
# =========================================================
protein_final_u <- protein_final %>%
  semi_join(retained_species_dates, by = c("Species_ID","Date","Group_For_Plots"))

cat("\n=== Universe enforcement ===\n")
cat("protein_final_u rows after semi_join:", nrow(protein_final_u), "\n")

# =========================================================
# 3) Compute TPF per Species×Date
#    Transporters: KEGG_Subcategory contains whole word "Transporters"
# =========================================================
protein_tagged <- protein_final_u %>%
  mutate(
    is_transporter = !is.na(KEGG_Subcategory) & str_detect(KEGG_Subcategory, "\\bTransporters\\b"),
    abund = Protein_Abundance_IBAQ_Norm
  )

species_date_tpf <- protein_tagged %>%
  group_by(Species_ID, Date, Group_For_Plots) %>%
  summarise(
    abund_total = sum(abund, na.rm = TRUE),
    abund_trans = sum(abund[is_transporter], na.rm = TRUE),
    TPF = abund_trans / abund_total,
    N_prot_total = n_distinct(.data[[protein_id_col]]),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  filter(is.finite(TPF), abund_total > 0, TPF > 0, TPF < 1, N_prot_total > 0)

cat("\n=== species_date_tpf ===\n")
cat("Rows:", nrow(species_date_tpf), "\n")

# =========================================================
# 4) Compute transporter-only KEGG intensity profiles per Species×Date
#    (summed iBAQ per KEGG_ID) and consecutive-date Bray–Curtis DISTANCE
# =========================================================
kegg_sd <- protein_tagged %>%
  filter(
    is_transporter,
    !is.na(KEGG_ID)
  ) %>%
  group_by(Species_ID, Date, KEGG_ID) %>%
  summarise(kegg_abund = sum(Protein_Abundance_IBAQ_Norm, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  filter(is.finite(kegg_abund), kegg_abund > 0)

compute_species_consecutive_braydist <- function(sp_id, kegg_sd_tbl, keep_dates_tbl) {

  dates <- keep_dates_tbl %>%
    filter(Species_ID == sp_id) %>%
    distinct(Date) %>%
    arrange(Date) %>%
    pull(Date)

  if (length(dates) < 2) return(NULL)

  mat_wide <- kegg_sd_tbl %>%
    filter(Species_ID == sp_id) %>%
    mutate(Date = as.character(Date)) %>%
    pivot_wider(names_from = KEGG_ID, values_from = kegg_abund, values_fill = 0) %>%
    filter(Date %in% as.character(dates)) %>%
    arrange(match(Date, as.character(dates)))

  if (nrow(mat_wide) < 2) return(NULL)

  M <- as.matrix(mat_wide %>% select(-Species_ID, -Date))
  rownames(M) <- mat_wide$Date

  pairs <- data.frame(
    Date1 = dates[-length(dates)],
    Date2 = dates[-1],
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )

  out <- lapply(seq_len(nrow(pairs)), function(i) {
    d1 <- as.character(pairs$Date1[i])
    d2 <- as.character(pairs$Date2[i])

    bc <- vegan::vegdist(M[c(d1, d2), , drop = FALSE], method = "bray")
    bc_dist <- as.numeric(bc)  # 0..1; lower=more similar (stable)

    data.frame(
      Species_ID = sp_id,
      Date1 = pairs$Date1[i],
      Date2 = pairs$Date2[i],
      bray_dist = bc_dist,
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    )
  })

  bind_rows(out)
}

keep_dates <- species_date_tpf %>% select(Species_ID, Date, Group_For_Plots) %>% distinct()

all_species <- sort(unique(keep_dates$Species_ID))

bray_transitions <- bind_rows(lapply(
  all_species,
  compute_species_consecutive_braydist,
  kegg_sd_tbl = kegg_sd,
  keep_dates_tbl = keep_dates
))

cat("\n=== bray_transitions ===\n")
cat("Rows:", nrow(bray_transitions), "\n")

# =========================================================
# 5) Species-level medians (ONE DOT PER SPECIES)
#    - median_TPF across dates
#    - median_bray across consecutive transitions
#    - median proteins detected (size covariate)
#    - taxonomy + highlight label
# =========================================================
species_covars <- species_date_tpf %>%
  group_by(Species_ID, Group_For_Plots) %>%
  summarise(
    n_dates = n(),
    median_TPF = median(TPF, na.rm = TRUE),
    median_prot_count = as.integer(round(median(N_prot_total, na.rm = TRUE))),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

species_bray <- bray_transitions %>%
  group_by(Species_ID) %>%
  summarise(
    n_transitions = n(),
    median_bray = median(bray_dist, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

analysis_df <- species_covars %>%
  left_join(species_bray, by = "Species_ID") %>%
  filter(
    is.finite(n_dates), n_dates >= 5,
    is.finite(median_prot_count), median_prot_count > 0,
    is.finite(median_TPF), median_TPF > 0, median_TPF < 1,
    is.finite(median_bray), median_bray >= 0, median_bray <= 1
  ) %>%
  left_join(
    species_taxonomy %>% select(Species_ID, Phylum, Genus) %>% distinct(),
    by = "Species_ID"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    Group_For_Plots = factor(Group_For_Plots, levels = trophic_order),
    Genus2 = ifelse(Genus %in% c("Limnohabitans", "Limnohabitans_A"), "Limnohabitans", Genus),
    highlight_label = case_when(
      Genus2 == "Flavobacterium" ~ "Flavobacterium",
      Genus2 == "Planktophila"   ~ "Planktophila",
      Genus2 == "Fonsibacter"    ~ "Fonsibacter",
      Phylum == "Planctomycetota" ~ "Planctomycetota (phy)",
      TRUE ~ "Rest"
    ),
    highlight_label = factor(highlight_label, levels = highlight_levels)
  ) %>%
  distinct(Species_ID, .keep_all = TRUE)

if (anyDuplicated(analysis_df$Species_ID) > 0) stop("Duplicate Species_ID in analysis_df (unexpected).")

cat("\n=== MEDIAN vs MEDIAN analysis_df ===\n")
cat("Total N dots:", nrow(analysis_df), "\n")
cat("N per trophic category:\n"); print(table(analysis_df$Group_For_Plots))
cat("N per trophic x highlight:\n"); print(analysis_df %>% count(Group_For_Plots, highlight_label))

# =========================================================
# 6) Figure A (median vs median): scatter + marginals
#     X: median TPF
#     Y: median variability (Bray–Curtis dissimilarity)
#     Axes fixed 0..1 by 0.25
#     Inference: logit(y) ~ log10(x)
# =========================================================
eps <- 1e-6
analysis_df <- analysis_df %>%
  mutate(
    x_med = median_TPF,
    y_med = pmin(pmax(median_bray, eps), 1 - eps),
    logit_y = qlogis(y_med),
    log10_x = log10(x_med)
  )

m_logit <- lm(logit_y ~ log10_x, data = analysis_df)
b <- coef(summary(m_logit))
beta  <- b["log10_x", "Estimate"]
pval  <- b["log10_x", "Pr(>|t|)"]
ci    <- confint(m_logit, "log10_x", level = 0.95)
ci_lo <- ci[1]; ci_hi <- ci[2]
N     <- nrow(analysis_df)

ann_txt <- paste0(
  "Inference (logit–log model):\n",
  "logit(BC) ~ log10(median TPF)\n",
  "β = ", round(beta, 3),
  "  95% CI [", round(ci_lo, 3), ", ", round(ci_hi, 3), "]\n",
  "p = ", fmt_p(pval), "   N = ", N
)

# Size legend breaks (hardcoded style used previously: min + 1/3 + 2/3 + max)
size_breaks <- analysis_df %>%
  summarise(
    mn = as.integer(round(min(median_prot_count, na.rm = TRUE))),
    q1 = as.integer(round(quantile(median_prot_count, 1/3, na.rm = TRUE))),
    q2 = as.integer(round(quantile(median_prot_count, 2/3, na.rm = TRUE))),
    mx = as.integer(round(max(median_prot_count, na.rm = TRUE)))
  ) %>%
  unlist(use.names = FALSE) %>%
  unique() %>%
  sort()

if (length(size_breaks) < 4) {
  mn <- min(analysis_df$median_prot_count, na.rm = TRUE)
  mx <- max(analysis_df$median_prot_count, na.rm = TRUE)
  size_breaks <- unique(as.integer(round(seq(mn, mx, length.out = 4))))
}

size_labels <- as.character(size_breaks)
size_labels[1] <- paste0(size_labels[1], " (min)")
size_labels[length(size_labels)] <- paste0(size_labels[length(size_labels)], " (max)")

pA_base <- ggplot(analysis_df, aes(x = x_med, y = median_bray, color = Group_For_Plots)) +
  geom_point(aes(size = median_prot_count), alpha = 0.85) +
  geom_smooth(
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    data = analysis_df,
    aes(x = x_med, y = median_bray),
    method = "lm",
    se = TRUE,
    color = "black",
    linewidth = 0.9
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = trophic_cols,
    drop = FALSE,
    labels = c("Oligo-like", "Intermediate", "Copio-like")
  ) +
  scale_size_continuous(
    name   = "Median proteins detected (count)",
    range  = c(2, 10),
    breaks = size_breaks,
    labels = size_labels
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 1), breaks = axis_breaks_01) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 1), breaks = axis_breaks_01) +
  labs(
    title = "Higher transporter investment predicts functional stability",
    subtitle = "Lower Bray–Curtis dissimilarity indicates higher stability; inference uses logit(dissimilarity) ~ log10(TPF)",
    x = "Median transporter proteome fraction (TPF)",
    y = "Median variability (Bray–Curtis dissimilarity)",
    color = "Trophic strategy"
  ) +
  annotate(
    "label",
    x = Inf, y = Inf,
    hjust = 1.02, vjust = 1.02,
    label = ann_txt,
    size = 3.2,
    label.size = 0.25
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    legend.position = "right"
  ) +
  theme(aspect.ratio = 1)

pA_noleg <- pA_base + theme(legend.position = "none")
pA_marg <- ggMarginal(
  pA_noleg,
  type = "density",
  margins = "both",
  groupColour = TRUE,
  groupFill   = TRUE,
  alpha = 0.25,
  size = 4
)
gA_plot <- grid::grid.grabExpr(print(pA_marg))

gA_size  <- get_legend(pA_base + guides(color = "none"))
gA_color <- get_legend(pA_base + guides(size = "none"))

A_leg_col <- arrangeGrob(
  gA_size,
  gA_color,
  ncol = 1,
  heights = c(1.2, 0.8)
)

FIG_A_MEDMED <- arrangeGrob(
  gA_plot,
  A_leg_col,
  ncol = 2,
  widths = c(3.3, 1.2)
)

grid.newpage(); grid.draw(FIG_A_MEDMED)
export_grob_pdf(FIG_A_MEDMED, base = "Figure_A_MEDIANxMEDIAN_BrayCurtisDissim_vs_TPF_A4_landscape",
                width = 11.69, height = 8.27)

# =========================================================
# 7) Figure B/C (median only): stacked boxplots + highlight legend
#     Axes fixed 0..1 by 0.25
# =========================================================
star <- function(p) {
  if (is.na(p)) return("ns")
  if (p < 1e-4) "****" else
    if (p < 1e-3) "***" else
      if (p < 1e-2) "**" else
        if (p < 5e-2) "*" else "ns"
}
pairwise_wilcox_tidy <- function(x, g) {
  pw <- pairwise.wilcox.test(x, g, p.adjust.method = "BH", exact = FALSE)
  m <- pw$p.value
  out <- data.frame()
  if (!is.null(m)) {
    rn <- rownames(m); cn <- colnames(m)
    for (i in seq_along(rn)) for (j in seq_along(cn)) {
      p <- m[i, j]
      if (!is.na(p)) out <- rbind(out, data.frame(group1 = rn[i], group2 = cn[j], p_adj = p))
    }
  }
  out
}
get_pw <- function(tbl, a, b) {
  p <- tbl$p_adj[(tbl$group1 == a & tbl$group2 == b) | (tbl$group1 == b & tbl$group2 == a)]
  if (length(p) == 0) NA_real_ else p[1]
}

# inference on log10(values) as before (reviewer-safe); clamp to avoid -Inf
analysis_df <- analysis_df %>%
  mutate(
    log_med_tpf  = log10(pmin(pmax(median_TPF, eps), 1 - eps)),
    log_med_bray = log10(pmin(pmax(median_bray, eps), 1 - eps))
  )

kw_med_tpf <- kruskal.test(log_med_tpf ~ Group_For_Plots, data = analysis_df)
pw_med_tpf <- pairwise_wilcox_tidy(analysis_df$log_med_tpf, analysis_df$Group_For_Plots)
p_med_OI <- get_pw(pw_med_tpf, "Oligo_like", "Intermediate")
p_med_OC <- get_pw(pw_med_tpf, "Oligo_like", "Copio_like")
p_med_IC <- get_pw(pw_med_tpf, "Intermediate", "Copio_like")

kw_med_bray <- kruskal.test(log_med_bray ~ Group_For_Plots, data = analysis_df)
pw_med_bray <- pairwise_wilcox_tidy(analysis_df$log_med_bray, analysis_df$Group_For_Plots)
p_bray_OI <- get_pw(pw_med_bray, "Oligo_like", "Intermediate")
p_bray_OC <- get_pw(pw_med_bray, "Oligo_like", "Copio_like")
p_bray_IC <- get_pw(pw_med_bray, "Intermediate", "Copio_like")

# IMPORTANT: do NOT set coord_cartesian() inside this function;
# we control headroom in the plot with coord_cartesian(ylim=..., clip="off").
add_sig_brackets <- function(p, y_max, p12, p13, p23,
                             mults = c(1.10, 1.22, 1.34),
                             tick_frac = 0.03,
                             text_mult = 1.015) {
  t <- y_max * tick_frac
  p +
    geom_segment(aes(x=1, xend=2, y=y_max*mults[1], yend=y_max*mults[1]), inherit.aes=FALSE) +
    geom_segment(aes(x=1, xend=1, y=y_max*mults[1]-t, yend=y_max*mults[1]), inherit.aes=FALSE) +
    geom_segment(aes(x=2, xend=2, y=y_max*mults[1]-t, yend=y_max*mults[1]), inherit.aes=FALSE) +
    annotate("text", x=1.5, y=y_max*mults[1]*text_mult, label=star(p12), size=4) +
    geom_segment(aes(x=1, xend=3, y=y_max*mults[2], yend=y_max*mults[2]), inherit.aes=FALSE) +
    geom_segment(aes(x=1, xend=1, y=y_max*mults[2]-t, yend=y_max*mults[2]), inherit.aes=FALSE) +
    geom_segment(aes(x=3, xend=3, y=y_max*mults[2]-t, yend=y_max*mults[2]), inherit.aes=FALSE) +
    annotate("text", x=2.0, y=y_max*mults[2]*text_mult, label=star(p13), size=4) +
    geom_segment(aes(x=2, xend=3, y=y_max*mults[3], yend=y_max*mults[3]), inherit.aes=FALSE) +
    geom_segment(aes(x=2, xend=2, y=y_max*mults[3]-t, yend=y_max*mults[3]), inherit.aes=FALSE) +
    geom_segment(aes(x=3, xend=3, y=y_max*mults[3]-t, yend=y_max*mults[3]), inherit.aes=FALSE) +
    annotate("text", x=2.5, y=y_max*mults[3]*text_mult, label=star(p23), size=4)
}

jit <- position_jitter(width = 0.3, height = 0, seed = 1)

clean_theme <- theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    axis.title.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major = element_line(linewidth = 0.25),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = NA, colour = NA),
    plot.background  = element_rect(fill = NA, colour = NA),
    legend.position = "none",
    # extra headroom so brackets/stars are never clipped by the grob layout
    plot.margin = margin(28, 10, 10, 10)
  )

pt_size <- 8
pt_stroke <- 0.35

# Headroom for brackets (keeps axis ticks 0..1 but allows drawing above 1)
ylim_headroom <- c(0, 1.18)

# B: median TPF
pB <- ggplot(analysis_df, aes(x = Group_For_Plots, y = median_TPF)) +
  geom_boxplot(width = 0.60, outlier.shape = NA, fill = NA, color = "black", linewidth = 0.5) +
  geom_point(
    aes(fill = highlight_label),
    position = jit,
    shape = 21, color = "black",
    stroke = pt_stroke, alpha = 0.9, size = pt_size
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = highlight_cols, drop = FALSE) +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = axis_breaks_01, expand = c(0,0)) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = ylim_headroom, clip = "off") +
  labs(
    title = "B  Median transporter allocation differs among trophic strategies",
    subtitle = paste0("Inference on log10(values): Kruskal–Wallis p = ", fmt_p(kw_med_tpf$p.value)),
    y = "Median transporter proteome fraction (TPF)"
  ) +
  clean_theme

yBmax <- 1
pB <- add_sig_brackets(pB, yBmax, p_med_OI, p_med_OC, p_med_IC,
                       mults = c(1.06, 1.11, 1.16), tick_frac = 0.02, text_mult = 1.01)

# C: median Bray–Curtis dissimilarity (variability)
pC <- ggplot(analysis_df, aes(x = Group_For_Plots, y = median_bray)) +
  geom_boxplot(width = 0.60, outlier.shape = NA, fill = NA, color = "black", linewidth = 0.5) +
  geom_point(
    aes(fill = highlight_label),
    position = jit,
    shape = 21, color = "black",
    stroke = pt_stroke, alpha = 0.9, size = pt_size
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = highlight_cols, drop = FALSE) +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = axis_breaks_01, expand = c(0,0)) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = ylim_headroom, clip = "off") +
  labs(
    title = "C  Functional variability differs among trophic strategies",
    subtitle = paste0("Inference on log10(values): Kruskal–Wallis p = ", fmt_p(kw_med_bray$p.value)),
    y = "Median variability (Bray–Curtis dissimilarity)"
  ) +
  clean_theme

yCmax <- 1
pC <- add_sig_brackets(pC, yCmax, p_bray_OI, p_bray_OC, p_bray_IC,
                       mults = c(1.06, 1.11, 1.16), tick_frac = 0.02, text_mult = 1.01)

# Shared highlight legend (single column)
legend_df <- data.frame(highlight_label = factor(highlight_levels, levels = highlight_levels))

pLEG <- ggplot(legend_df, aes(x = highlight_label, y = 1, fill = highlight_label)) +
  geom_point(shape = 21, size = 5, color = "black", stroke = 0.5) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = highlight_cols, drop = FALSE) +
  guides(fill = guide_legend(title = NULL, ncol = 1, byrow = TRUE)) +
  theme_void() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "right",
    legend.text = element_text(size = 9),
    legend.spacing.y = unit(0.3, "lines")
  )

gLEG <- get_legend(pLEG)

BC_stack <- arrangeGrob(
  ggplotGrob(pB),
  ggplotGrob(pC),
  ncol = 1,
  heights = c(1, 1)
)

FIG_BC_MEDMED <- arrangeGrob(
  BC_stack,
  gLEG,
  ncol = 2,
  widths = c(3.0, 1.0)
)

grid.newpage(); grid.draw(FIG_BC_MEDMED)
export_grob_pdf(FIG_BC_MEDMED, base = "Figure_BC_MEDIANxMEDIAN_BrayCurtisDissim_and_TPF_A4_landscape",
                width = 11.69, height = 8.27)

# Final sanity prints
cat("\n=== FINAL SANITY (median vs median) ===\n")
cat("Total dots:", nrow(analysis_df), "\n")
cat("Dots per trophic:\n"); print(table(analysis_df$Group_For_Plots))
cat("Trophic x highlight:\n"); print(analysis_df %>% count(Group_For_Plots, highlight_label))
```

# mean tpf vs mean bray curtis dissimilarity
```{r}
############################################################
# MEAN vs MEAN ONLY
# - x: mean TPF per species
# - y: mean variability = mean Bray–Curtis dissimilarity
#      (lower = more stable; higher = more variable)
# - Hardcoded axes: 0..1 by 0.25 for scatter + boxplots
# - Same plotting style + highlight legend + PDF export only
############################################################

suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(dplyr)
  library(stringr)
  library(tidyr)
  library(ggplot2)
  library(scales)
  library(ggExtra)
  library(grid)
  library(gridExtra)
  library(readr)
  library(vegan)
})

# =========================================================
# 0) Safety checks — objects expected from your load block
# =========================================================
required_objects <- c("protein_final", "retained_species_dates", "species_taxonomy")
for (obj in required_objects) {
  if (!exists(obj, inherits = TRUE)) stop("Object not found in environment: ", obj)
}

req_keys_cols <- c("Species_ID","Date","Group_For_Plots")
missing_keys <- setdiff(req_keys_cols, names(retained_species_dates))
if (length(missing_keys) > 0) stop("retained_species_dates missing: ", paste(missing_keys, collapse=", "))

req_tax_cols <- c("Species_ID","Phylum","Genus")
missing_tax <- setdiff(req_tax_cols, names(species_taxonomy))
if (length(missing_tax) > 0) stop("species_taxonomy missing: ", paste(missing_tax, collapse=", "))

req_prot_cols <- c("Species_ID","Date","Group_For_Plots","Protein_Abundance_IBAQ_Norm","KEGG_Subcategory","KEGG_ID")
missing_prot <- setdiff(req_prot_cols, names(protein_final))
if (length(missing_prot) > 0) stop("protein_final missing: ", paste(missing_prot, collapse=", "))

protein_id_col <- dplyr::case_when(
  "Protein_Group" %in% names(protein_final) ~ "Protein_Group",
  "Protein_ID"    %in% names(protein_final) ~ "Protein_ID",
  TRUE ~ NA_character_
)
if (is.na(protein_id_col)) stop("protein_final must have Protein_Group or Protein_ID for counting detected proteins.")

protein_final <- protein_final %>% mutate(Date = as.Date(Date))
retained_species_dates <- retained_species_dates %>% mutate(Date = as.Date(Date))

# =========================================================
# 1) Fixed settings (orders, colors, highlight scheme)
# =========================================================
trophic_order <- c("Oligo_like", "Intermediate", "Copio_like")
trophic_cols <- c(
  "Oligo_like"   = "#93C2E6",
  "Intermediate" = "#EDEAE9",
  "Copio_like"   = "#BA5205"
)

highlight_cols <- c(
  "Flavobacterium"        = "#E6BE1E",
  "Planktophila"          = "#ADD5E4",
  "Fonsibacter"           = "#C6FC05",
  "Planctomycetota (phy)" = "#7B3294",
  "Rest"                  = "grey"
)
highlight_levels <- names(highlight_cols)

axis_breaks_01 <- seq(0, 1, by = 0.25)

fmt_p <- function(p) {
  if (is.na(p)) return("NA")
  if (p < 1e-3) "<0.001" else format(p, digits = 2, scientific = TRUE)
}

get_legend <- function(p) {
  g <- ggplotGrob(p)
  idx <- which(sapply(g$grobs, function(x) x$name) == "guide-box")
  if (length(idx) != 1) stop("Legend extraction failed.")
  g$grobs[[idx]]
}

export_grob_pdf <- function(g, base, width, height) {
  pdf(paste0(base, ".pdf"), width = width, height = height)
  grid.newpage(); grid.draw(g)
  dev.off()
  cat("\nSaved PDF:\n  ", normalizePath(paste0(base, ".pdf")), "\n", sep="")
}

# =========================================================
# 2) Enforce Species×Date×Group universe (critical)
# =========================================================
protein_final_u <- protein_final %>%
  semi_join(retained_species_dates, by = c("Species_ID","Date","Group_For_Plots"))

cat("\n=== Universe enforcement ===\n")
cat("protein_final_u rows after semi_join:", nrow(protein_final_u), "\n")

# =========================================================
# 3) Compute TPF per Species×Date
#    Transporters: KEGG_Subcategory contains whole word "Transporters"
# =========================================================
protein_tagged <- protein_final_u %>%
  mutate(
    is_transporter = !is.na(KEGG_Subcategory) & str_detect(KEGG_Subcategory, "\\bTransporters\\b"),
    abund = Protein_Abundance_IBAQ_Norm
  )

species_date_tpf <- protein_tagged %>%
  group_by(Species_ID, Date, Group_For_Plots) %>%
  summarise(
    abund_total = sum(abund, na.rm = TRUE),
    abund_trans = sum(abund[is_transporter], na.rm = TRUE),
    TPF = abund_trans / abund_total,
    N_prot_total = n_distinct(.data[[protein_id_col]]),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  filter(is.finite(TPF), abund_total > 0, TPF > 0, TPF < 1, N_prot_total > 0)

cat("\n=== species_date_tpf ===\n")
cat("Rows:", nrow(species_date_tpf), "\n")

# =========================================================
# 4) Compute transporter-only KEGG intensity profiles per Species×Date
#    (summed iBAQ per KEGG_ID) and consecutive-date Bray–Curtis DISTANCE
# =========================================================
kegg_sd <- protein_tagged %>%
  filter(
    is_transporter,
    !is.na(KEGG_ID)
  ) %>%
  group_by(Species_ID, Date, KEGG_ID) %>%
  summarise(kegg_abund = sum(Protein_Abundance_IBAQ_Norm, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  filter(is.finite(kegg_abund), kegg_abund > 0)

compute_species_consecutive_braydist <- function(sp_id, kegg_sd_tbl, keep_dates_tbl) {

  dates <- keep_dates_tbl %>%
    filter(Species_ID == sp_id) %>%
    distinct(Date) %>%
    arrange(Date) %>%
    pull(Date)

  if (length(dates) < 2) return(NULL)

  mat_wide <- kegg_sd_tbl %>%
    filter(Species_ID == sp_id) %>%
    mutate(Date = as.character(Date)) %>%
    pivot_wider(names_from = KEGG_ID, values_from = kegg_abund, values_fill = 0) %>%
    filter(Date %in% as.character(dates)) %>%
    arrange(match(Date, as.character(dates)))

  if (nrow(mat_wide) < 2) return(NULL)

  M <- as.matrix(mat_wide %>% select(-Species_ID, -Date))
  rownames(M) <- mat_wide$Date

  pairs <- data.frame(
    Date1 = dates[-length(dates)],
    Date2 = dates[-1],
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )

  out <- lapply(seq_len(nrow(pairs)), function(i) {
    d1 <- as.character(pairs$Date1[i])
    d2 <- as.character(pairs$Date2[i])

    bc <- vegan::vegdist(M[c(d1, d2), , drop = FALSE], method = "bray")
    bc_dist <- as.numeric(bc)

    data.frame(
      Species_ID = sp_id,
      Date1 = pairs$Date1[i],
      Date2 = pairs$Date2[i],
      bray_dist = bc_dist,
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    )
  })

  bind_rows(out)
}

keep_dates <- species_date_tpf %>% select(Species_ID, Date, Group_For_Plots) %>% distinct()
all_species <- sort(unique(keep_dates$Species_ID))

bray_transitions <- bind_rows(lapply(
  all_species,
  compute_species_consecutive_braydist,
  kegg_sd_tbl = kegg_sd,
  keep_dates_tbl = keep_dates
))

cat("\n=== bray_transitions ===\n")
cat("Rows:", nrow(bray_transitions), "\n")

# =========================================================
# 5) Species-level means (ONE DOT PER SPECIES)
#    - mean_TPF across dates
#    - mean_bray across consecutive transitions
#    - mean proteins detected (size covariate)
#    - taxonomy + highlight label
# =========================================================
species_covars <- species_date_tpf %>%
  group_by(Species_ID, Group_For_Plots) %>%
  summarise(
    n_dates = n(),
    mean_TPF = mean(TPF, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_prot_count = as.integer(round(mean(N_prot_total, na.rm = TRUE))),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

species_bray <- bray_transitions %>%
  group_by(Species_ID) %>%
  summarise(
    n_transitions = n(),
    mean_bray = mean(bray_dist, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

analysis_df <- species_covars %>%
  left_join(species_bray, by = "Species_ID") %>%
  filter(
    is.finite(n_dates), n_dates >= 5,
    is.finite(mean_prot_count), mean_prot_count > 0,
    is.finite(mean_TPF), mean_TPF > 0, mean_TPF < 1,
    is.finite(mean_bray), mean_bray >= 0, mean_bray <= 1
  ) %>%
  left_join(
    species_taxonomy %>% select(Species_ID, Phylum, Genus) %>% distinct(),
    by = "Species_ID"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    Group_For_Plots = factor(Group_For_Plots, levels = trophic_order),
    Genus2 = ifelse(Genus %in% c("Limnohabitans", "Limnohabitans_A"), "Limnohabitans", Genus),
    highlight_label = case_when(
      Genus2 == "Flavobacterium" ~ "Flavobacterium",
      Genus2 == "Planktophila"   ~ "Planktophila",
      Genus2 == "Fonsibacter"    ~ "Fonsibacter",
      Phylum == "Planctomycetota" ~ "Planctomycetota (phy)",
      TRUE ~ "Rest"
    ),
    highlight_label = factor(highlight_label, levels = highlight_levels)
  ) %>%
  distinct(Species_ID, .keep_all = TRUE)

if (anyDuplicated(analysis_df$Species_ID) > 0) stop("Duplicate Species_ID in analysis_df (unexpected).")

cat("\n=== MEAN vs MEAN analysis_df ===\n")
cat("Total N dots:", nrow(analysis_df), "\n")
cat("N per trophic category:\n"); print(table(analysis_df$Group_For_Plots))
cat("N per trophic x highlight:\n"); print(analysis_df %>% count(Group_For_Plots, highlight_label))

# =========================================================
# 6) Figure A (mean vs mean): scatter + marginals
#     X: mean TPF
#     Y: mean variability (Bray–Curtis dissimilarity)
#     Axes fixed 0..1 by 0.25
#     Inference: logit(y) ~ log10(x)
# =========================================================
eps <- 1e-6
analysis_df <- analysis_df %>%
  mutate(
    x_mean = mean_TPF,
    y_mean = pmin(pmax(mean_bray, eps), 1 - eps),
    logit_y = qlogis(y_mean),
    log10_x = log10(x_mean)
  )

m_logit <- lm(logit_y ~ log10_x, data = analysis_df)
b <- coef(summary(m_logit))
beta  <- b["log10_x", "Estimate"]
pval  <- b["log10_x", "Pr(>|t|)"]
ci    <- confint(m_logit, "log10_x", level = 0.95)
ci_lo <- ci[1]; ci_hi <- ci[2]
N     <- nrow(analysis_df)

ann_txt <- paste0(
  "Inference (logit–log model):\n",
  "logit(BC) ~ log10(mean TPF)\n",
  "β = ", round(beta, 3),
  "  95% CI [", round(ci_lo, 3), ", ", round(ci_hi, 3), "]\n",
  "p = ", fmt_p(pval), "   N = ", N
)

# Size legend breaks (hardcoded style used previously: min + 1/3 + 2/3 + max)
size_breaks <- analysis_df %>%
  summarise(
    mn = as.integer(round(min(mean_prot_count, na.rm = TRUE))),
    q1 = as.integer(round(quantile(mean_prot_count, 1/3, na.rm = TRUE))),
    q2 = as.integer(round(quantile(mean_prot_count, 2/3, na.rm = TRUE))),
    mx = as.integer(round(max(mean_prot_count, na.rm = TRUE)))
  ) %>%
  unlist(use.names = FALSE) %>%
  unique() %>%
  sort()

if (length(size_breaks) < 4) {
  mn <- min(analysis_df$mean_prot_count, na.rm = TRUE)
  mx <- max(analysis_df$mean_prot_count, na.rm = TRUE)
  size_breaks <- unique(as.integer(round(seq(mn, mx, length.out = 4))))
}

size_labels <- as.character(size_breaks)
size_labels[1] <- paste0(size_labels[1], " (min)")
size_labels[length(size_labels)] <- paste0(size_labels[length(size_labels)], " (max)")

pA_base <- ggplot(analysis_df, aes(x = x_mean, y = mean_bray, color = Group_For_Plots)) +
  geom_point(aes(size = mean_prot_count), alpha = 0.85) +
  geom_smooth(
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    data = analysis_df,
    aes(x = x_mean, y = mean_bray),
    method = "lm",
    se = TRUE,
    color = "black",
    linewidth = 0.9
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = trophic_cols,
    drop = FALSE,
    labels = c("Oligo-like", "Intermediate", "Copio-like")
  ) +
  scale_size_continuous(
    name   = "Mean proteins detected (count)",
    range  = c(2, 10),
    breaks = size_breaks,
    labels = size_labels
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 1), breaks = axis_breaks_01) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 1), breaks = axis_breaks_01) +
  labs(
    title = "Higher transporter investment predicts functional stability",
    subtitle = "Lower Bray–Curtis dissimilarity indicates higher stability; inference uses logit(dissimilarity) ~ log10(TPF)",
    x = "Mean transporter proteome fraction (TPF)",
    y = "Mean variability (Bray–Curtis dissimilarity)",
    color = "Trophic strategy"
  ) +
  annotate(
    "label",
    x = Inf, y = Inf,
    hjust = 1.02, vjust = 1.02,
    label = ann_txt,
    size = 3.2,
    label.size = 0.25
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    legend.position = "right"
  ) +
  theme(aspect.ratio = 1)

pA_noleg <- pA_base + theme(legend.position = "none")
pA_marg <- ggMarginal(
  pA_noleg,
  type = "density",
  margins = "both",
  groupColour = TRUE,
  groupFill   = TRUE,
  alpha = 0.25,
  size = 4
)
gA_plot <- grid::grid.grabExpr(print(pA_marg))

gA_size  <- get_legend(pA_base + guides(color = "none"))
gA_color <- get_legend(pA_base + guides(size = "none"))

A_leg_col <- arrangeGrob(
  gA_size,
  gA_color,
  ncol = 1,
  heights = c(1.2, 0.8)
)

FIG_A_MEANMEAN <- arrangeGrob(
  gA_plot,
  A_leg_col,
  ncol = 2,
  widths = c(3.3, 1.2)
)

# grid.newpage(); grid.draw(FIG_A_MEANMEAN)
# export_grob_pdf(FIG_A_MEANMEAN, base = "Figure_A_MEANxMEAN_BrayCurtisDissim_vs_TPF_A4_landscape",
#                 width = 11.69, height = 8.27)

# =========================================================
# 7) Figure B/C (mean only): stacked boxplots + highlight legend
#     Axes fixed 0..1 by 0.25
# =========================================================
add_sig_brackets <- function(p, y_max, p12, p13, p23,
                             mults = c(1.10, 1.22, 1.34),
                             tick_frac = 0.03,
                             text_mult = 1.015) {
  t <- y_max * tick_frac
  p +
    geom_segment(aes(x=1, xend=2, y=y_max*mults[1], yend=y_max*mults[1]), inherit.aes=FALSE) +
    geom_segment(aes(x=1, xend=1, y=y_max*mults[1]-t, yend=y_max*mults[1]), inherit.aes=FALSE) +
    geom_segment(aes(x=2, xend=2, y=y_max*mults[1]-t, yend=y_max*mults[1]), inherit.aes=FALSE) +
    annotate("text", x=1.5, y=y_max*mults[1]*text_mult, label=star(p12), size=4) +
    geom_segment(aes(x=1, xend=3, y=y_max*mults[2], yend=y_max*mults[2]), inherit.aes=FALSE) +
    geom_segment(aes(x=1, xend=1, y=y_max*mults[2]-t, yend=y_max*mults[2]), inherit.aes=FALSE) +
    geom_segment(aes(x=3, xend=3, y=y_max*mults[2]-t, yend=y_max*mults[2]), inherit.aes=FALSE) +
    annotate("text", x=2.0, y=y_max*mults[2]*text_mult, label=star(p13), size=4) +
    geom_segment(aes(x=2, xend=3, y=y_max*mults[3], yend=y_max*mults[3]), inherit.aes=FALSE) +
    geom_segment(aes(x=2, xend=2, y=y_max*mults[3]-t, yend=y_max*mults[3]), inherit.aes=FALSE) +
    geom_segment(aes(x=3, xend=3, y=y_max*mults[3]-t, yend=y_max*mults[3]), inherit.aes=FALSE) +
    annotate("text", x=2.5, y=y_max*mults[3]*text_mult, label=star(p23), size=4)
}

jit <- position_jitter(width = 0.3, height = 0, seed = 1)

clean_theme <- theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    axis.title.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major = element_line(linewidth = 0.25),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = NA, colour = NA),
    plot.background  = element_rect(fill = NA, colour = NA),
    legend.position = "none",
    plot.margin = margin(28, 10, 10, 10)
  )

pt_size <- 8
pt_stroke <- 0.35
ylim_headroom <- c(0, 1.18)

analysis_df <- analysis_df %>%
  mutate(
    log_mean_tpf  = log10(pmin(pmax(mean_TPF, eps), 1 - eps)),
    log_mean_bray = log10(pmin(pmax(mean_bray, eps), 1 - eps))
  )

kw_mean_tpf <- kruskal.test(log_mean_tpf ~ Group_For_Plots, data = analysis_df)
pw_mean_tpf <- pairwise_wilcox_tidy(analysis_df$log_mean_tpf, analysis_df$Group_For_Plots)
p_mean_OI <- get_pw(pw_mean_tpf, "Oligo_like", "Intermediate")
p_mean_OC <- get_pw(pw_mean_tpf, "Oligo_like", "Copio_like")
p_mean_IC <- get_pw(pw_mean_tpf, "Intermediate", "Copio_like")

kw_mean_bray <- kruskal.test(log_mean_bray ~ Group_For_Plots, data = analysis_df)
pw_mean_bray <- pairwise_wilcox_tidy(analysis_df$log_mean_bray, analysis_df$Group_For_Plots)
p_mbray_OI <- get_pw(pw_mean_bray, "Oligo_like", "Intermediate")
p_mbray_OC <- get_pw(pw_mean_bray, "Oligo_like", "Copio_like")
p_mbray_IC <- get_pw(pw_mean_bray, "Intermediate", "Copio_like")

pB <- ggplot(analysis_df, aes(x = Group_For_Plots, y = mean_TPF)) +
  geom_boxplot(width = 0.60, outlier.shape = NA, fill = NA, color = "black", linewidth = 0.5) +
  geom_point(
    aes(fill = highlight_label),
    position = jit,
    shape = 21, color = "black",
    stroke = pt_stroke, alpha = 0.9, size = pt_size
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = highlight_cols, drop = FALSE) +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = axis_breaks_01, expand = c(0,0)) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = ylim_headroom, clip = "off") +
  labs(
    title = "B  Mean transporter allocation differs among trophic strategies",
    subtitle = paste0("Inference on log10(values): Kruskal–Wallis p = ", fmt_p(kw_mean_tpf$p.value)),
    y = "Mean transporter proteome fraction (TPF)"
  ) +
  clean_theme

pB <- add_sig_brackets(pB, 1, p_mean_OI, p_mean_OC, p_mean_IC,
                       mults = c(1.06, 1.11, 1.16), tick_frac = 0.02, text_mult = 1.01)

pC <- ggplot(analysis_df, aes(x = Group_For_Plots, y = mean_bray)) +
  geom_boxplot(width = 0.60, outlier.shape = NA, fill = NA, color = "black", linewidth = 0.5) +
  geom_point(
    aes(fill = highlight_label),
    position = jit,
    shape = 21, color = "black",
    stroke = pt_stroke, alpha = 0.9, size = pt_size
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = highlight_cols, drop = FALSE) +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = axis_breaks_01, expand = c(0,0)) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = ylim_headroom, clip = "off") +
  labs(
    title = "C  Functional variability differs among trophic strategies",
    subtitle = paste0("Inference on log10(values): Kruskal–Wallis p = ", fmt_p(kw_mean_bray$p.value)),
    y = "Mean variability (Bray–Curtis dissimilarity)"
  ) +
  clean_theme

pC <- add_sig_brackets(pC, 1, p_mbray_OI, p_mbray_OC, p_mbray_IC,
                       mults = c(1.06, 1.11, 1.16), tick_frac = 0.02, text_mult = 1.01)

legend_df <- data.frame(highlight_label = factor(highlight_levels, levels = highlight_levels))
pLEG <- ggplot(legend_df, aes(x = highlight_label, y = 1, fill = highlight_label)) +
  geom_point(shape = 21, size = 5, color = "black", stroke = 0.5) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = highlight_cols, drop = FALSE) +
  guides(fill = guide_legend(title = NULL, ncol = 1, byrow = TRUE)) +
  theme_void() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "right",
    legend.text = element_text(size = 9),
    legend.spacing.y = unit(0.3, "lines")
  )
gLEG <- get_legend(pLEG)

BC_stack <- arrangeGrob(
  ggplotGrob(pB),
  ggplotGrob(pC),
  ncol = 1,
  heights = c(1, 1)
)

FIG_BC_MEANMEAN <- arrangeGrob(
  BC_stack,
  gLEG,
  ncol = 2,
  widths = c(3.0, 1.0)
)

# grid.newpage(); grid.draw(FIG_BC_MEANMEAN)
# export_grob_pdf(FIG_BC_MEANMEAN, base = "Figure_BC_MEANxMEAN_BrayCurtisDissim_and_TPF_A4_landscape",
#                 width = 11.69, height = 8.27)

cat("\n=== FINAL SANITY (mean vs mean) ===\n")
cat("Total dots:", nrow(analysis_df), "\n")
cat("Dots per trophic:\n"); print(table(analysis_df$Group_For_Plots))
cat("Trophic x highlight:\n"); print(analysis_df %>% count(Group_For_Plots, highlight_label))
```


```{r}
############################################################
# Main Figures
#
#
# INPUT OBJECTS (assumed already loaded in your session):
#   - protein_final
#   - retained_species_dates
#   - species_taxonomy
#
# OUTPUT:
#   - Figure_A_MEANxMEAN_BrayCurtisDissim_vs_TPF_A4_landscape.pdf
#   - Figure_BC_MEANxMEAN_BrayCurtisDissim_and_TPF_A4_landscape.pdf
#
# CORE IDEA
#   Fig A:
#     - One dot = one species
#     - x = mean transporter proteome fraction (TPF)
#     - y = mean variability (mean Bray–Curtis dissimilarity across consecutive dates)
#     - Marginal densities grouped by trophic strategy (as in your original)
#     - Dot color scheme:
#         * keep the 3 trophic groups
#         * WITHIN each group: gradient by mean trophic index
#           (darker = lower trophic index, lighter = higher)
#     - Curve:
#         * We show the model-implied curve from the INFERENCE model
#           logit(BC) ~ log10(TPF)
#         * The curve is predicted on the transformed scale and then
#           back-transformed to BC scale via plogis()
#         * NO confidence ribbon is shown (to avoid mixing scales visually)
#     - NO dot-size encoding (proteins now shown in Fig B/C)
#
#   Fig B/C:
#     - Boxplots by trophic strategy
#     - Overlaid jittered points:
#         * fill = highlight taxon group
#         * size = mean proteins detected per species (mean across its dates)
#     - Right-side combined legend includes:
#         * Mean proteins detected (size legend)
#         * Highlight colors legend
#
############################################################

suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(dplyr)
  library(stringr)
  library(tidyr)
  library(ggplot2)
  library(scales)
  library(ggExtra)
  library(grid)
  library(gridExtra)
  library(vegan)
})

# =========================================================
# Helper utilities (plot export, legends, stats helpers)
# =========================================================

export_grob_pdf <- function(g, base, width, height) {
  pdf(paste0(base, ".pdf"), width = width, height = height)
  grid.newpage(); grid.draw(g)
  dev.off()
  cat("\nSaved PDF:\n  ", normalizePath(paste0(base, ".pdf")), "\n", sep = "")
}

get_legend <- function(p) {
  g <- ggplotGrob(p)
  idx <- which(sapply(g$grobs, function(x) x$name) == "guide-box")
  if (length(idx) != 1) stop("Legend extraction failed.")
  g$grobs[[idx]]
}

fmt_p <- function(p) {
  if (is.na(p)) return("NA")
  if (p < 1e-3) "<0.001" else format(p, digits = 2, scientific = TRUE)
}

star <- function(p) {
  if (is.na(p)) return("NA")
  if (p < 1e-4) "****"
  else if (p < 1e-3) "***"
  else if (p < 1e-2) "**"
  else if (p < 5e-2) "*"
  else "ns"
}

pairwise_wilcox_tidy <- function(x, g, p_adjust = "BH") {
  g <- droplevels(as.factor(g))
  pw <- pairwise.wilcox.test(x, g, p.adjust.method = p_adjust, exact = FALSE)
  M  <- pw$p.value
  if (is.null(M)) return(tibble(g1=character(), g2=character(), p=double()))
  as.data.frame(as.table(M), stringsAsFactors = FALSE) %>%
    rename(g1 = Var1, g2 = Var2, p = Freq) %>%
    filter(!is.na(p)) %>%
    tibble::as_tibble()
}

get_pw <- function(pw_tbl, a, b) {
  hit1 <- pw_tbl %>% filter(g1 == a, g2 == b)
  hit2 <- pw_tbl %>% filter(g1 == b, g2 == a)
  if (nrow(hit1) == 1) return(hit1$p)
  if (nrow(hit2) == 1) return(hit2$p)
  NA_real_
}

add_sig_brackets <- function(p, y_max, p12, p13, p23,
                             mults = c(1.10, 1.22, 1.34),
                             tick_frac = 0.03,
                             text_mult = 1.015) {
  t <- y_max * tick_frac
  p +
    geom_segment(aes(x=1, xend=2, y=y_max*mults[1], yend=y_max*mults[1]), inherit.aes=FALSE) +
    geom_segment(aes(x=1, xend=1, y=y_max*mults[1]-t, yend=y_max*mults[1]), inherit.aes=FALSE) +
    geom_segment(aes(x=2, xend=2, y=y_max*mults[1]-t, yend=y_max*mults[1]), inherit.aes=FALSE) +
    annotate("text", x=1.5, y=y_max*mults[1]*text_mult, label=star(p12), size=4) +
    geom_segment(aes(x=1, xend=3, y=y_max*mults[2], yend=y_max*mults[2]), inherit.aes=FALSE) +
    geom_segment(aes(x=1, xend=1, y=y_max*mults[2]-t, yend=y_max*mults[2]), inherit.aes=FALSE) +
    geom_segment(aes(x=3, xend=3, y=y_max*mults[2]-t, yend=y_max*mults[2]), inherit.aes=FALSE) +
    annotate("text", x=2.0, y=y_max*mults[2]*text_mult, label=star(p13), size=4) +
    geom_segment(aes(x=2, xend=3, y=y_max*mults[3], yend=y_max*mults[3]), inherit.aes=FALSE) +
    geom_segment(aes(x=2, xend=2, y=y_max*mults[3]-t, yend=y_max*mults[3]), inherit.aes=FALSE) +
    geom_segment(aes(x=3, xend=3, y=y_max*mults[3]-t, yend=y_max*mults[3]), inherit.aes=FALSE) +
    annotate("text", x=2.5, y=y_max*mults[3]*text_mult, label=star(p23), size=4)
}

# =========================================================
# Fixed settings (orders, colors, highlight scheme)
# =========================================================

trophic_order <- c("Oligo_like", "Intermediate", "Copio_like")

# Base colors (highest trophic index within group -> these colors)
trophic_cols <- c(
  "Oligo_like"   = "#93C2E6",
  "Intermediate" = "#EDEAE9",
  "Copio_like"   = "#BA5205"
)

# Dark endpoint colors (lowest trophic index within group -> these colors)
trophic_cols_dark <- c(
  "Oligo_like"   = "#1F4E79",
  "Intermediate" = "#4D4D4D",
  "Copio_like"   = "#5A2A0C"
)

highlight_cols <- c(
  "Flavobacterium"        = "#E6BE1E",
  "Planktophila"          = "#ADD5E4",
  "Fonsibacter"           = "#C6FC05",
  "Planctomycetota (phy)" = "#7B3294",
  "Rest"                  = "grey"
)
highlight_levels <- names(highlight_cols)

axis_breaks_01 <- seq(0, 1, by = 0.25)

# =========================================================
# 1) Enforce Species×Date×Group universe (critical)
# =========================================================

protein_final <- protein_final %>% mutate(Date = as.Date(Date))
retained_species_dates <- retained_species_dates %>% mutate(Date = as.Date(Date))

protein_final_u <- protein_final %>%
  semi_join(retained_species_dates, by = c("Species_ID","Date","Group_For_Plots"))

# =========================================================
# 2) Identify protein identifier column (Protein_Group preferred)
# =========================================================
protein_id_col <- if ("Protein_Group" %in% names(protein_final_u)) "Protein_Group" else "Protein_ID"

# =========================================================
# 3) Compute TPF per Species×Date
#    Transporters: KEGG_Subcategory contains whole word "Transporters"
# =========================================================

protein_tagged <- protein_final_u %>%
  mutate(
    is_transporter = !is.na(KEGG_Subcategory) & str_detect(KEGG_Subcategory, "\\bTransporters\\b"),
    abund = Protein_Abundance_IBAQ_Norm
  )

species_date_tpf <- protein_tagged %>%
  group_by(Species_ID, Date, Group_For_Plots) %>%
  summarise(
    abund_total = sum(abund, na.rm = TRUE),
    abund_trans = sum(abund[is_transporter], na.rm = TRUE),
    TPF = abund_trans / abund_total,
    N_prot_total = n_distinct(.data[[protein_id_col]]),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  filter(is.finite(TPF), abund_total > 0, TPF > 0, TPF < 1, N_prot_total > 0)

# =========================================================
# 4) Transporter-only KEGG profiles and consecutive Bray–Curtis distances
# =========================================================

kegg_sd <- protein_tagged %>%
  filter(is_transporter, !is.na(KEGG_ID)) %>%
  group_by(Species_ID, Date, KEGG_ID) %>%
  summarise(kegg_abund = sum(Protein_Abundance_IBAQ_Norm, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  filter(is.finite(kegg_abund), kegg_abund > 0)

compute_species_consecutive_braydist <- function(sp_id, kegg_sd_tbl, keep_dates_tbl) {

  dates <- keep_dates_tbl %>%
    filter(Species_ID == sp_id) %>%
    distinct(Date) %>%
    arrange(Date) %>%
    pull(Date)

  if (length(dates) < 2) return(NULL)

  mat_wide <- kegg_sd_tbl %>%
    filter(Species_ID == sp_id) %>%
    mutate(Date = as.character(Date)) %>%
    pivot_wider(names_from = KEGG_ID, values_from = kegg_abund, values_fill = 0) %>%
    filter(Date %in% as.character(dates)) %>%
    arrange(match(Date, as.character(dates)))

  if (nrow(mat_wide) < 2) return(NULL)

  M <- as.matrix(mat_wide %>% select(-Species_ID, -Date))
  rownames(M) <- mat_wide$Date

  pairs <- data.frame(
    Date1 = dates[-length(dates)],
    Date2 = dates[-1],
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )

  out <- lapply(seq_len(nrow(pairs)), function(i) {
    d1 <- as.character(pairs$Date1[i])
    d2 <- as.character(pairs$Date2[i])
    bc <- vegan::vegdist(M[c(d1, d2), , drop = FALSE], method = "bray")

    data.frame(
      Species_ID = sp_id,
      Date1 = pairs$Date1[i],
      Date2 = pairs$Date2[i],
      bray_dist = as.numeric(bc),
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    )
  })

  bind_rows(out)
}

keep_dates <- species_date_tpf %>% select(Species_ID, Date, Group_For_Plots) %>% distinct()
all_species <- sort(unique(keep_dates$Species_ID))

bray_transitions <- bind_rows(lapply(
  all_species,
  compute_species_consecutive_braydist,
  kegg_sd_tbl = kegg_sd,
  keep_dates_tbl = keep_dates
))

# =========================================================
# 5) Species-level means (ONE DOT PER SPECIES)
#    - mean TPF across dates
#    - mean Bray distance across consecutive transitions
#    - mean proteins detected across dates (for B/C dot sizes)
#    - mean trophic index across all records of the species
#    - taxonomy + highlight label
# =========================================================

species_covars <- species_date_tpf %>%
  group_by(Species_ID, Group_For_Plots) %>%
  summarise(
    n_dates = n(),
    mean_TPF = mean(TPF, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_prot_count = as.integer(round(mean(N_prot_total, na.rm = TRUE))),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

species_bray <- bray_transitions %>%
  group_by(Species_ID) %>%
  summarise(
    n_transitions = n(),
    mean_bray = mean(bray_dist, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )
```
